You are auditing source code for an INDEPENDENT external code review (client: Telus). Tools: Bash, Read.

STEP 0 — SKIP IF DONE. Run: test -s /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_37.tsv && echo DONE || echo TODO
If it prints DONE, this shard is already complete — reply exactly "shard 37: cached" and STOP. Do nothing else.

STEP 1 — get your file list: /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; print(chr(10).join(json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[37]))"
It prints lines "relpath:START-END" relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent.

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every assigned file/range line by line with the Read tool (offset/limit from START-END; use limit up to 2000 per call to minimize calls). Do NOT grep-and-skim — read the code.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (file/module, class, func/method, var, const, enum value) that looks UNPROFESSIONAL in an external review:
  - marketing/quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best
  - temporal/version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(meaning 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes/informal/vague/slang: junk, garbage, hack, spy, stub(as a real name), yolo, stuff, misc, foo/bar, doit, thing, blah, landmine, nuke, magic-number-as-word, terse cryptic abbreviations
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME.** Only KEEP-BUT-NOTED for unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a REAL domain term genuinely implemented (finalize, proper_noun, evict_older_than, SmartArt only if it truly makes SmartArt).

STEP 4 — APPEND TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_37.tsv (one per flagged name), using printf with real tabs:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY<TAB>SUGGESTED_NAME
  VERDICT=RENAME or KEEP-NOTED; KIND=file/class/func/var/const/enum. If a file is fully clean, write: NONE<TAB>-<TAB>relpath<TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-

Reply ONLY: "shard 37: <R> renames, <K> kept across <F> files".

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	"""I-deepfix-001 Wave-3 T6 — the two-layer citation RENDER policy (single source of truth).
2	
3	This module fixes the body/appendix + Layer-1/Layer-2 citation boundary in ONE place so the render
4	fixes that consume it (T1 basket->multi-cite, T2 cited-reference-list typing, T5 audit-machinery ->
5	appendix, F1 residual-coverage placement) all read the SAME policy instead of re-deriving it and
6	drifting. It is a pure RENDER-layer typing of an already-verified basket; it NEVER verifies, gates,
7	promotes, drops, caps, or thins a source.
8	
9	WHAT THE POLICY DECIDES (and, just as importantly, what it does NOT):
10	
11	  1. THE TWO TYPED CITATION LAYERS (readability/attribution, NOT a scoring device).
12	     Given a verified basket, ``split_basket_citation_layers`` types its distinct-origin ``SUPPORTS``
13	     members into:
14	       * Layer 1 = the inline BODY citation(s) whose span grounds THAT clause. Normally exactly ONE
15	         (the load-bearing representative); up to ``PG_CITATION_LAYER1_MAX`` (default 1, licensed
16	         cross-source analytical sentence -> 2 per D2). The composer may pass the exact grounding
17	         ``evidence_id``s it wrote via ``load_bearing_ev_ids``; absent that, Layer 1 defaults to the
18	         top-credibility-weight distinct-origin member (the natural single ``[N]`` representative).
19	       * Layer 2 = a typed per-claim corroboration line citing EVERY OTHER distinct-origin ``SUPPORTS``
20	         member (keep-all, DNA CONSOLIDATE). Same-origin duplicates NEVER render as a second citation.
21	     THE HARD INVARIANT (asserted by the fail-loud test): Layer1 ev_ids and Layer2 ev_ids PARTITION
22	     the full distinct-origin ``SUPPORTS`` ev_id set — union == the whole set, intersection == empty.
23	     Nothing is dropped; to the DeepTRACE scorer, Layer-1 union Layer-2 is ONE citation set (so #8
24	     thoroughness is high because every supporting source is cited, and #7 accuracy stays high because
25	     ONLY genuinely-supporting sources are cited). The two layers are a READABILITY split, so #6
26	     source-necessity (min-vertex-cover over the support matrix) is UNAFFECTED by the layer render.
27	
28	  2. THE BODY / APPENDIX / DEMOTE BOUNDARY (fixed ONCE, jointly with T5 + F1).
29	     ``classify_render_block`` maps a render-block KIND to exactly one destination:
30	       * ``BODY``   — a relevant, on-topic, strict_verify-passing CLAIM (section prose, key findings,
31	         abstract, conclusion, AND F1 residual-coverage sentences — they are relevant statements).
32	       * ``APPENDIX`` — audit / disclosure / weight MACHINERY (reliability header, corroborated-
33	         weighted-findings roll-up, weight-basis labels, count-reconciliation prose, the credibility
34	         ledger, the per-claim Source-corroboration section, bibliography-audit). Kept + disclosed,
35	         relocated OUT of the scored body so the DeepTRACE #3 relevant-statement denominator is not
36	         diluted by POLARIS's own machinery. NEVER deleted.
37	       * ``DEMOTE_DISCLOSE`` — a confirmed-off-topic residual basket, routed to the F3 demote-and-
38	         disclose tail (kept + disclosed, never in the scored body).
39	     The distinct-origin corroboration line of a body claim (Layer 2) stays WITH its claim in the
40	     BODY — the appendix move relocates only non-claim audit machinery, never a claim's own citations.
41	
42	DNA (CLAUDE.md §-1.3): WEIGHT don't FILTER; CONSOLIDATE don't DROP. This policy is the CONSOLIDATE
43	leg made visible: it keeps ALL distinct-origin corroborators as real citations and only TYPES where
44	each one renders. It adds no cap/target/thinner and touches no faithfulness state. The >=2-origin
45	basket floor and strict_verify remain the only hard gates, untouched.
46	
47	LAW VI (zero hard-coding): every knob comes from the environment —
48	  * ``PG_CITATION_TWO_LAYER_POLICY``  kill-switch (default ON). OFF => the policy collapses to a
49	    single Layer-1 citation and an EMPTY Layer-2 (legacy single-``[N]`` behaviour), so callers can
50	    fall back byte-compatibly.
51	  * ``PG_CITATION_LAYER1_MAX``        max inline body citations (default 1; 2 licenses a cross-source
52	    analytical sentence). Overflow load-bearing members do NOT vanish — they fall to Layer 2 (still
53	    cited, keep-all), so the partition invariant always holds.
54	  * ``PG_CITATION_APPENDIX_KINDS``    comma-list of EXTRA block kinds to force into the appendix
55	    (additive to the canonical audit-machinery set below).
56	
57	PURE: no network, no model, no faithfulness-file import, no input mutation. snake_case throughout.
58	"""
59	from __future__ import annotations
60	
61	import os
62	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
63	from enum import Enum
64	from typing import Any, Iterable, Optional
65	
66	# ── LAW VI env knobs ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
67	_TWO_LAYER_ENV = "PG_CITATION_TWO_LAYER_POLICY"       # kill-switch, default ON
68	_LAYER1_MAX_ENV = "PG_CITATION_LAYER1_MAX"            # inline body citations, default 1
69	_APPENDIX_KINDS_ENV = "PG_CITATION_APPENDIX_KINDS"    # extra appendix kinds (comma-list)
70	
71	_DEFAULT_LAYER1_MAX = 1
72	# The cross-source analytical sentence (D2) is the only licensed 2-citation body clause; the layer-1
73	# cap is never allowed below 1 (a body claim always carries at least its load-bearing citation).
74	_MIN_LAYER1_MAX = 1
75	
76	
77	def two_layer_policy_enabled() -> bool:
78	    """True iff the two-layer citation render is active (LAW VI kill-switch ``PG_CITATION_TWO_LAYER_POLICY``).
79	
80	    DEFAULT-ON: the policy is a faithfulness-neutral render typing, so it is on by default; ``=0`` (or
81	    ``off``/``false``/``no``) collapses to a single inline citation with an empty corroboration layer
82	    (legacy single-``[N]`` behaviour) for a byte-compatible fallback."""
83	    raw = os.getenv(_TWO_LAYER_ENV, "1").strip().lower()
84	    return raw not in ("", "0", "false", "off", "no")
85	
86	
87	def layer1_max() -> int:
88	    """Max inline BODY (Layer-1) citations per claim (LAW VI ``PG_CITATION_LAYER1_MAX``, default 1).
89	
90	    Floored at 1 (a body claim always carries at least its load-bearing citation). A malformed value
91	    falls back to the default rather than failing the render."""
92	    raw = os.getenv(_LAYER1_MAX_ENV, str(_DEFAULT_LAYER1_MAX)).strip()
93	    try:
94	        val = int(raw)
95	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
96	        return _DEFAULT_LAYER1_MAX
97	    return val if val >= _MIN_LAYER1_MAX else _MIN_LAYER1_MAX
98	
99	
100	# ── the two typed citation layers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
101	
102	
103	def _member_attr(member: Any, key: str) -> Any:
104	    """Read ``key`` from a member that is EITHER a dataclass/object (attribute) OR a plain ``dict``
105	    (the shape the ``_basket_corroboration_block`` render iterates). One accessor so the object path
106	    (ClaimBasket/BasketMember) and the dict path (bibliography ``row['baskets']`` members) agree."""
107	    if isinstance(member, dict):
108	        return member.get(key)
109	    return getattr(member, key, None)
110	
111	
112	def _member_ev_id(member: Any) -> str:
113	    """The member's evidence_id as a string (empty when absent). Dict- and object-aware."""
114	    return str(_member_attr(member, "evidence_id") or "")
115	
116	
117	def _member_origin(member: Any) -> str:
118	    """The member's ORIGIN identity for distinct-origin dedupe: ``origin_cluster_id`` (fallback
119	    ``evidence_id``, then object id). Matches ``verified_compose._distinct_origin_supports`` exactly so
120	    the render layers and the multi-cite composer agree on what 'one distinct source' means. Dict- and
121	    object-aware."""
122	    return str(
123	        _member_attr(member, "origin_cluster_id") or _member_attr(member, "evidence_id") or id(member)
124	    )
125	
126	
127	def _member_weight(member: Any) -> float:
128	    """The member's credibility WEIGHT for representative selection (highest-weight = Layer-1). Prefers
129	    ``credibility_weight`` (the verified_compose ordering key), falls back to ``authority_score``; 0.0
130	    when neither is a number. Dict- and object-aware. PURE."""
131	    for key in ("credibility_weight", "authority_score"):
132	        v = _member_attr(member, key)
133	        if isinstance(v, (int, float)) and not isinstance(v, bool):
134	            return float(v)
135	    return 0.0
136	
137	
138	def _distinct_origin_supports(basket: Any) -> list[Any]:
139	    """The basket's ``SUPPORTS`` members deduped to ONE per distinct origin, highest credibility weight
140	    first. Delegates to the proven ``verified_compose`` helper so there is exactly ONE definition of
141	    'distinct-origin SUPPORTS' in the codebase; the import is local to avoid a module-load cycle."""
142	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.verified_compose import (  # noqa: PLC0415
143	        _distinct_origin_supports as _vc_distinct_origin_supports,
144	    )
145	    return list(_vc_distinct_origin_supports(basket))
146	
147	
148	@dataclass(frozen=True)
149	class CitationLayers:
150	    """The typed two-layer citation split for ONE verified basket.
151	
152	    ``layer1_members`` — the inline BODY citation member(s) (load-bearing span; normally 1, <=layer1_max).
153	    ``layer2_members`` — the per-claim corroboration-line member(s): every OTHER distinct-origin SUPPORTS
154	                         member (keep-all).
155	    The two member lists PARTITION the basket's distinct-origin SUPPORTS set (union == whole, no overlap,
156	    no same-origin duplicate). ``enabled`` records whether the two-layer render was active."""
157	
158	    layer1_members: list[Any] = field(default_factory=list)
159	    layer2_members: list[Any] = field(default_factory=list)
160	    enabled: bool = True
161	
162	    @property
163	    def layer1_ev_ids(self) -> list[str]:
164	        return [_member_ev_id(m) for m in self.layer1_members]
165	
166	    @property
167	    def layer2_ev_ids(self) -> list[str]:
168	        return [_member_ev_id(m) for m in self.layer2_members]
169	
170	    @property
171	    def cited_ev_ids(self) -> list[str]:
172	        """The FULL cited set (Layer-1 ∪ Layer-2), body order — what the DeepTRACE parser sees as one
173	        citation set for this claim, and what T2 lists as a real numbered reference."""
174	        return self.layer1_ev_ids + self.layer2_ev_ids
175	
176	    @property
177	    def cited_members(self) -> list[Any]:
178	        return list(self.layer1_members) + list(self.layer2_members)
179	
180	
181	def _distinct_origin_from_members(members: Iterable[Any]) -> list[Any]:
182	    """Dedupe an EXPLICIT SUPPORTS member list to ONE per distinct ORIGIN, highest credibility weight
183	    kept, order-stable by weight desc. Dict- OR object-aware (unlike ``_distinct_origin_supports``,
184	    which delegates to the object-only ``verified_compose`` helper). Used by the ``supports_members``
185	    overload so the render's dict basket-members flow through the SAME partition logic. PURE."""
186	    ordered = sorted(list(members or []), key=_member_weight, reverse=True)
187	    seen: set[str] = set()
188	    out: list[Any] = []
189	    for m in ordered:
190	        origin = _member_origin(m)
191	        if origin in seen:
192	            continue
193	        seen.add(origin)
194	        out.append(m)
195	    return out
196	
197	
198	def _partition_supports(
199	    supports: list[Any], load_bearing_ev_ids: Optional[Iterable[str]], enabled: bool
200	) -> CitationLayers:
201	    """Partition an ALREADY distinct-origin, weight-desc-ordered SUPPORTS list into the two layers.
202	    The single implementation both ``split_basket_citation_layers`` entry points share, so the object
203	    (ClaimBasket) path and the explicit-members render path can never drift. PURE."""
204	    if not supports:
205	        return CitationLayers(layer1_members=[], layer2_members=[], enabled=enabled)
206	    if not enabled:
207	        # Legacy single-``[N]`` behaviour: one inline citation, no corroboration line.
208	        return CitationLayers(layer1_members=[supports[0]], layer2_members=[], enabled=False)
209	
210	    max_l1 = layer1_max()
211	    # Resolve the load-bearing member(s) in the supplied weight-desc order (deterministic, stable).
212	    load_set: set[str] = {str(e) for e in (load_bearing_ev_ids or []) if str(e)}
213	    if load_set:
214	        primary = [m for m in supports if _member_ev_id(m) in load_set]
215	        # Guard: a caller-supplied ev_id that is NOT a distinct-origin SUPPORTS member is ignored (the
216	        # policy never invents a citation) — supports already excludes non-SUPPORTS and same-origin dups.
217	        if not primary:
218	            primary = [supports[0]]
219	    else:
220	        primary = [supports[0]]
221	
222	    layer1 = primary[:max_l1]
223	    layer1_origins = {_member_origin(m) for m in layer1}
224	    # Layer 2 = every OTHER distinct-origin member (the load-bearing overflow beyond the cap included).
225	    layer2 = [m for m in supports if _member_origin(m) not in layer1_origins]
226	    return CitationLayers(layer1_members=layer1, layer2_members=layer2, enabled=True)
227	
228	
229	def split_basket_citation_layers(
230	    basket: Any = None,
231	    *,
232	    supports_members: Optional[Iterable[Any]] = None,
233	    load_bearing_ev_ids: Optional[Iterable[str]] = None,
234	) -> CitationLayers:
235	    """Type a verified basket's distinct-origin ``SUPPORTS`` members into the two citation layers.
236	
237	    Two ways to call — the SAME policy, one named function (the production render path and the
238	    object tests both land here):
239	      * ``split_basket_citation_layers(basket)`` — the object path: read the ``SUPPORTS`` members off
240	        a ``ClaimBasket`` (via the shared ``verified_compose`` distinct-origin helper).
241	      * ``split_basket_citation_layers(supports_members=[...])`` — the RENDER path: the caller
242	        (``_basket_corroboration_block``) has ALREADY selected the verified SUPPORTS members (dicts);
243	        dedupe-to-distinct-origin here (dict-aware) and partition. This is what makes the policy a
244	        genuine render-path consumer, not a test-only helper.
245	
246	    Layer 1 (inline body) = the load-bearing citation(s). If ``load_bearing_ev_ids`` is given (the
247	    ev_ids the composer actually grounded the body clause on), those distinct-origin members lead
248	    Layer 1, capped at ``layer1_max()``; otherwise Layer 1 defaults to the single top-weight
249	    distinct-origin member (the natural single-``[N]`` representative). Layer 2 = EVERY remaining
250	    distinct-origin SUPPORTS member (keep-all corroboration, DNA CONSOLIDATE). Overflow load-bearing
251	    members beyond the Layer-1 cap fall to Layer 2 (still cited) so the partition is total.
252	
253	    HARD GUARANTEE: ``layer1 ∪ layer2`` == the distinct-origin SUPPORTS ev_id set, and
254	    ``layer1 ∩ layer2`` == empty. Nothing dropped, no same-origin duplicate double-cited. When the
255	    kill-switch is OFF the render collapses to a single Layer-1 citation and an EMPTY Layer-2 (legacy)."""
256	    if supports_members is not None:
257	        supports = _distinct_origin_from_members(supports_members)
258	    else:
259	        supports = _distinct_origin_supports(basket)
260	    return _partition_supports(supports, load_bearing_ev_ids, two_layer_policy_enabled())
261	
262	
263	# ── the body / appendix / demote render boundary (fixed ONCE, jointly with T5 + F1) ────────────────
264	
265	
266	class RenderDestination(str, Enum):
267	    """Where a render block lands in the DeepTRACE-scored output."""
268	
269	    BODY = "body"                        # a relevant, on-topic, verified CLAIM (scored)
270	    APPENDIX = "appendix"                # audit / disclosure / weight MACHINERY (kept + disclosed)
271	    DEMOTE_DISCLOSE = "demote_disclose"  # confirmed-off-topic residual -> F3 tail (kept + disclosed)
272	
273	
274	# Canonical BODY block kinds — relevant on-topic verified CLAIMS. F1 residual-coverage sentences are
275	# BODY (they are relevant statements per the Wave-3 T5/F1 joint placement decision).
276	_BODY_KINDS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
277	    "section_prose",
278	    "key_findings",
279	    "abstract",
280	    "conclusion",
281	    "residual_coverage",          # F1: keep-all per-facet residual verified sentences ARE relevant
282	    "claim",
283	    "corroboration_line",         # Layer-2 corroboration line stays WITH its body claim
284	})
285	
286	# Canonical APPENDIX block kinds — POLARIS's own audit / disclosure / weight MACHINERY. Non-claim
287	# statements the GPT-5 decomposer would otherwise count in the #3 relevant-statement denominator.
288	_APPENDIX_KINDS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
289	    "reliability_header",
290	    "corroborated_weighted_findings",
291	    "weight_basis",
292	    "count_reconciliation",
293	    "credibility_ledger",
294	    "source_corroboration_rollup",
295	    "disclosure",
296	    "bibliography_audit",
297	    "methods",
298	})
299	
300	# Canonical DEMOTE kinds — confirmed-off-topic residual routed to the F3 demote-and-disclose tail.
301	_DEMOTE_KINDS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
302	    "off_topic_residual",
303	})
304	
305	
306	def _extra_appendix_kinds() -> frozenset[str]:
307	    """LAW VI ``PG_CITATION_APPENDIX_KINDS``: extra kinds forced into the appendix (comma-list)."""
308	    raw = os.getenv(_APPENDIX_KINDS_ENV, "").strip()
309	    if not raw:
310	        return frozenset()
311	    return frozenset(k.strip().lower() for k in raw.split(",") if k.strip())
312	
313	
314	def classify_render_block(kind: str) -> RenderDestination:
315	    """Map a render-block KIND to its single canonical destination (the body/appendix/demote boundary,
316	    defined here ONCE for T5 + F1 + T1/T2). Unknown kinds default to ``BODY`` (fail-open toward the
317	    scored body: a real claim must never be silently hidden from the reader; only NAMED audit machinery
318	    is relocated). The ``PG_CITATION_APPENDIX_KINDS`` override can additively force a kind to appendix."""
319	    k = (kind or "").strip().lower()
320	    if k in _extra_appendix_kinds():
321	        return RenderDestination.APPENDIX
322	    if k in _DEMOTE_KINDS:
323	        return RenderDestination.DEMOTE_DISCLOSE
324	    if k in _APPENDIX_KINDS:
325	        return RenderDestination.APPENDIX
326	    # BODY kinds and anything unrecognized render in the scored body (fail-open toward visibility).
327	    return RenderDestination.BODY
328	
329	
330	def is_audit_appendix_block(kind: str) -> bool:
331	    """Convenience predicate for T5: True iff ``kind`` is audit/disclosure/weight machinery that the
332	    render must relocate OUT of the scored body into the typed appendix."""
333	    return classify_render_block(kind) is RenderDestination.APPENDIX
334

content.text: This file is heavy with internal jargon (Wave-3, DeepTRACE, DNA, T1/T2/T5/F1 codes) but those are consistent domain terms in this codebase. Let me note issues: module docstring codes like "I-deepfix-001", cryptic references. Let's continue reading the rest.

content.content:
1	"""I-deepfix-001 Wave 2b (#1344) — the MINIMAL independently-entailing inline citation set.
2	
3	Design: ``.codex/I-deepfix-001/wave2b_brief.md`` + ``.codex/I-deepfix-001/REAL_PLAN_2026.md``
4	(``traceability`` items 1 + 2). MODULE ONLY — wiring into the render path is a separate Batch-2
5	step (``2b-wiring``); nothing here is called from the live pipeline yet.
6	
7	WHAT IT DECIDES (and, just as importantly, what it does NOT):
8	
9	Given ONE already-verified sentence and the basket members currently cited inline on it, decide
10	which members render as inline ``[N]`` citations and which move to the corroboration WEIGHT channel
11	(the ``_basket_corroboration_block`` / CWF surface). Two legs:
12	
13	  1. PRUNE (DeepTRACE Citation-Accuracy leg). For each member run
14	     ``entails_directional(premise=member_span, hypothesis=sentence)`` — the ALCE / DeepTRACE
15	     citation direction (the cited SPAN must entail the CLAIM). A confident ``False`` (the span
16	     does not carry that sentence) demotes the member out of the inline set into the weight channel.
17	     A ``True`` OR a ``None`` (infra-unavailable verdict) KEEPS the member — fail-open: a citation
18	     is never dropped on model uncertainty. An empty span => KEEP (cannot judge).
19	
20	  2. MVC-DEMOTE (DeepTRACE Source-Necessity leg). Among the survivors, a deterministic greedy
21	     set-cover / min-vertex-cover over the statement x source support matrix. The minimal COVER set
22	     (load-bearing sources — each uniquely covers a support atom) always stays inline. The remaining
23	     same-statement corroborators are MVC-redundant (their support is already covered); a TUNABLE
24	     threshold (``PG_MIN_CITE_SET_MAX_INLINE``) decides how many redundant corroborators stay inline;
25	     the rest move to the weight channel.
26	
27	NOT decided / NOT touched: no verdict is changed, no source is deleted, no basket is shrunk;
28	strict_verify / NLI / 4-role D8 / provenance / span-grounding are neither imported nor invoked. This
29	is a RENDER-CHANNEL placement decision only — inline-cite vs weight-channel.
30	
31	DNA (CLAUDE.md §-1.3): CONSOLIDATE-keep-all is preserved. The basket keeps ALL members; this only
32	relabels WHERE each renders. The two returned lists PARTITION the input (``inline`` disjoint-union
33	``weight`` == the input members; nothing lost). A member demoted to the weight channel is still a
34	real corroborator the CWF surface renders as count + tier weight (§-1.3 WEIGHT-not-citation).
35	
36	HONEST TRADEOFF: removing a TRUE independent supporter from the inline set LOWERS DeepTRACE
37	Citation-Thoroughness (#8) while raising Source-Necessity (#6). So the MVC demotion is
38	threshold-TUNED (``PG_MIN_CITE_SET_MAX_INLINE``), never forced to a strict singleton. The default
39	(``0``) DISABLES demotion (prune-only) — zero thoroughness loss.
40	
41	LAW VI (zero hard-coding): every knob comes from the environment (see the ``ENV_*`` table below).
42	PURE: no network, no faithfulness-file import at module load, no input mutation; the only model
43	touch is the injectable ``entail_fn`` seam (default lazily wraps ``entails_directional``), so
44	importing this module is cheap and the OFF identity path loads nothing.
45	"""
46	from __future__ import annotations
47	
48	import logging
49	import os
50	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
51	from typing import Any, Callable, Iterable, Mapping, Optional
52	
53	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.citation_set_minimizer")
54	
55	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
56	# LAW VI env knobs — all tunables are env-overridable; no hard-coded thresholds.
57	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
58	ENV_FLAG = "PG_MIN_CITE_SET"                 # master gate, default OFF
59	ENV_PRUNE = "PG_MIN_CITE_SET_PRUNE"          # prune-non-entailing leg, default ON (when master ON)
60	ENV_MAX_INLINE = "PG_MIN_CITE_SET_MAX_INLINE"  # MVC demotion cap; <=0 => demotion disabled
61	ENV_MARGIN = "PG_MIN_CITE_SET_MARGIN"        # entailment-logit margin forwarded to entails_directional
62	
63	_DEFAULT_MAX_INLINE = 0   # 0 => demotion DISABLED (prune-only); the zero-thoroughness-loss default
64	# The synthetic single support atom used when the caller supplies no per-source support map: every
65	# entailing survivor covers this ONE atom (= the whole statement), so the greedy cover is size 1 and
66	# every OTHER survivor is an MVC-redundant same-statement corroborator. A caller with a real
67	# report-level statement x source matrix passes ``support_of`` to get a genuine multi-atom cover.
68	_STATEMENT_ATOM = "__statement__"
69	
70	_FALSEY = ("", "0", "false", "off", "no")
71	
72	
73	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
74	# LAW VI flag readers
75	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
76	def min_cite_set_enabled() -> bool:
77	    """True iff the minimal-citation-set render is active (master gate ``PG_MIN_CITE_SET``).
78	
79	    DEFAULT-OFF: OFF => ``minimize_citation_set`` returns the identity no-op (every input member
80	    inline, empty weight channel), so wiring this module in later is byte-identical while OFF."""
81	    return os.getenv(ENV_FLAG, "0").strip().lower() not in _FALSEY
82	
83	
84	def _prune_enabled() -> bool:
85	    """True iff the prune-non-entailing leg runs (``PG_MIN_CITE_SET_PRUNE``, default ON when master ON)."""
86	    return os.getenv(ENV_PRUNE, "1").strip().lower() not in _FALSEY
87	
88	
89	def _max_inline() -> int:
90	    """MVC demotion cap (``PG_MIN_CITE_SET_MAX_INLINE``, default 0 => demotion disabled).
91	
92	    ``<=0`` disables demotion (prune-only). A malformed value falls back to the default rather than
93	    failing the render."""
94	    raw = os.getenv(ENV_MAX_INLINE, str(_DEFAULT_MAX_INLINE)).strip()
95	    try:
96	        return int(raw)
97	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
98	        return _DEFAULT_MAX_INLINE
99	
100	
101	def _margin() -> Optional[float]:
102	    """Entailment margin forwarded to ``entails_directional`` (``PG_MIN_CITE_SET_MARGIN``).
103	
104	    Blank / malformed => None (use the consolidation-NLI default margin)."""
105	    raw = os.getenv(ENV_MARGIN, "").strip()
106	    if not raw:
107	        return None
108	    try:
109	        return float(raw)
110	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
111	        return None
112	
113	
114	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
115	# Member-shape accessors — dict- AND object-aware, mirroring generator/citation_layer_policy.py
116	# so this module and the render policy agree on 'one distinct source' / 'span' / 'weight'.
117	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
118	def _member_attr(member: Any, key: str) -> Any:
119	    if isinstance(member, Mapping):
120	        return member.get(key)
121	    return getattr(member, key, None)
122	
123	
124	def _member_ev_id(member: Any) -> str:
125	    """The member's evidence_id as a string (empty when absent)."""
126	    return str(_member_attr(member, "evidence_id") or "")
127	
128	
129	def _member_weight(member: Any) -> float:
130	    """Credibility WEIGHT for representative selection (prefers ``credibility_weight``, then
131	    ``authority_score``; 0.0 when neither is a number). Booleans are NOT weights."""
132	    for key in ("credibility_weight", "authority_score"):
133	        v = _member_attr(member, key)
134	        if isinstance(v, (int, float)) and not isinstance(v, bool):
135	            return float(v)
136	    return 0.0
137	
138	
139	def _member_span(member: Any, spans: Optional[Mapping[str, str]]) -> str:
140	    """The member's cited SPAN text. Prefers an explicit ``spans[evidence_id]`` (the per-member
141	    spans the caller passes), else the member's own ``direct_quote`` (the verbatim span it was
142	    verified on). Empty string when neither is present (=> the prune leg cannot judge => KEEP)."""
143	    if spans is not None:
144	        eid = _member_ev_id(member)
145	        if eid and eid in spans:
146	            return str(spans.get(eid) or "")
147	    return str(_member_attr(member, "direct_quote") or "")
148	
149	
150	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
151	# Result
152	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
153	@dataclass(frozen=True)
154	class MinCiteResult:
155	    """The inline-vs-weight-channel placement decision for ONE verified sentence.
156	
157	    ``inline_members`` — the minimal independently-entailing inline ``[N]`` citation set.
158	    ``weight_members`` — every demoted member (rendered in the CWF corroboration weight channel);
159	                         == ``pruned_members`` ++ ``demoted_members``.
160	    ``pruned_members`` — transparency: members whose span did NOT entail the sentence.
161	    ``demoted_members`` — transparency: entailing survivors demoted as MVC-redundant.
162	    ``enabled`` — whether the minimizer ran (False on the OFF identity no-op).
163	
164	    HARD INVARIANT (keep-all): ``inline_members`` ⊎ ``weight_members`` == the input members
165	    (multiset, by identity), and the two lists are disjoint. Nothing is deleted.
166	
167	    NOTE: ``frozen=True`` freezes the field BINDINGS, not the list contents (the lists stay
168	    mutable). Treat every returned list as read-only — this module never mutates them post-return
169	    and callers should not either."""
170	
171	    inline_members: list = field(default_factory=list)
172	    weight_members: list = field(default_factory=list)
173	    pruned_members: list = field(default_factory=list)
174	    demoted_members: list = field(default_factory=list)
175	    enabled: bool = True
176	
177	    @property
178	    def all_members(self) -> list:
179	        """``inline ++ weight`` — the full input member set (keep-all)."""
180	        return list(self.inline_members) + list(self.weight_members)
181	
182	
183	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
184	# Default entailment seam (lazy — importing this module stays cheap; the OFF path never loads it)
185	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
186	def _default_entail_fn(margin: Optional[float]) -> Callable[[str, str], Optional[bool]]:
187	    """A ``(premise_span, hypothesis_sentence) -> Optional[bool]`` callable that lazily wraps the
188	    resident consolidation cross-encoder's ``entails_directional`` (ALCE/DeepTRACE span->claim
189	    direction). Lazy local import so module import is cheap and the OFF identity path loads no model.
190	    Returns None on any infra fault (caller keeps the citation)."""
191	
192	    def _fn(premise: str, hypothesis: str) -> Optional[bool]:
193	        # BOTH the lazy import AND the call live inside the try (I-deepfix-001 2b review P1): a
194	        # RUNTIME fault escaping ``entails_directional`` (e.g. a malformed-logits IndexError, or
195	        # future signature drift) must return None (KEEP the citation), NOT crash the prune loop.
196	        try:
197	            from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.consolidation_nli import (  # noqa: PLC0415
198	                entails_directional,
199	            )
200	            return entails_directional(premise, hypothesis, margin=margin)
201	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — import OR runtime fault => UNKNOWN => KEEP (fail-open)
202	            logger.warning(
203	                "[citation_set_minimizer] entails_directional unavailable/failed (%s); "
204	                "returning None (caller keeps the citation).", exc,
205	            )
206	            return None
207	
208	    return _fn
209	
210	
211	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
212	# Deterministic greedy set-cover / min-vertex-cover (index-based, order-independent)
213	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
214	def _greedy_set_cover(
215	    survivors: list, support_of: Callable[[Any], frozenset]
216	) -> tuple[set[int], list[int]]:
217	    """Return ``(cover_indices, redundant_indices)`` for a greedy minimal set-cover of the union of
218	    every survivor's support atoms.
219	
220	    Deterministic + order-independent: at each step pick the survivor covering the MOST still-
221	    uncovered atoms, tie-broken by credibility-weight desc then lowest index. Members whose support
222	    is empty can never be load-bearing (they cover nothing) => they fall to ``redundant``. A member
223	    is COVER (load-bearing) iff the greedy pass selected it to cover a still-uncovered atom;
224	    everything else is MVC-redundant (its support is already covered)."""
225	    n = len(survivors)
226	    supports = [frozenset(support_of(survivors[i]) or ()) for i in range(n)]
227	    all_atoms: set = set()
228	    for s in supports:
229	        all_atoms |= s
230	
231	    covered: set = set()
232	    cover: list[int] = []
233	    remaining = set(range(n))
234	    while covered != all_atoms:
235	        best: Optional[int] = None
236	        best_key: Optional[tuple] = None
237	        for i in sorted(remaining):
238	            gain = len(supports[i] - covered)
239	            if gain <= 0:
240	                continue
241	            key = (gain, _member_weight(survivors[i]))
242	            if best is None or key > best_key:  # type: ignore[operator]
243	                best, best_key = i, key
244	        if best is None:
245	            break  # no remaining member can cover the rest (cannot happen: all_atoms == union)
246	        cover.append(best)
247	        covered |= supports[best]
248	        remaining.discard(best)
249	
250	    cover_set = set(cover)
251	    redundant = [i for i in range(n) if i not in cover_set]
252	    return cover_set, redundant
253	
254	
255	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
256	# Entry point
257	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
258	def minimize_citation_set(
259	    sentence: str,
260	    members: Iterable[Any],
261	    *,
262	    spans: Optional[Mapping[str, str]] = None,
263	    support_of: Optional[Callable[[Any], frozenset]] = None,
264	    entail_fn: Optional[Callable[[str, str], Optional[bool]]] = None,
265	    max_inline: Optional[int] = None,
266	) -> MinCiteResult:
267	    """Decide inline-vs-weight-channel placement for ``members`` cited on ``sentence``.
268	
269	    ``spans`` — optional ``{evidence_id: span_text}``; falls back to each member's ``direct_quote``.
270	    ``support_of`` — optional ``member -> frozenset[atom]`` statement x source support; default is a
271	        single synthetic atom (whole-statement) so the cover is size 1 and every other survivor is an
272	        MVC-redundant same-statement corroborator.
273	    ``entail_fn`` — optional ``(premise_span, hypothesis_sentence) -> Optional[bool]`` seam
274	        (test-injection); default lazily wraps ``entails_directional``. ``None``/``True`` => KEEP,
275	        ``False`` => prune (fail-open on uncertainty).
276	    ``max_inline`` — per-call override of ``PG_MIN_CITE_SET_MAX_INLINE``.
277	
278	    OFF (master gate) => identity no-op: every input member inline, empty weight channel. Returns a
279	    ``MinCiteResult`` whose ``inline`` ⊎ ``weight`` == the input members (keep-all; nothing lost)."""
280	    members_list = list(members)
281	
282	    # OFF => byte-identical identity no-op (no import, no model, no reorder). Wiring later is a no-op
283	    # while OFF because callers get back exactly the members they passed, inline, in order.
284	    if not min_cite_set_enabled():
285	        return MinCiteResult(
286	            inline_members=members_list,
287	            weight_members=[],
288	            pruned_members=[],
289	            demoted_members=[],
290	            enabled=False,
291	        )
292	
293	    # ── Leg 1: PRUNE non-entailing spans (fail-open on None / empty / uncertainty). ──────────────
294	    survivors: list = []
295	    pruned: list = []
296	    if _prune_enabled() and sentence and sentence.strip():
297	        efn = entail_fn if entail_fn is not None else _default_entail_fn(_margin())
298	        for m in members_list:
299	            span = _member_span(m, spans)
300	            if not span.strip():
301	                survivors.append(m)  # no span => cannot judge => KEEP
302	                continue
303	            # Defense-in-depth (I-deepfix-001 2b review P1): the entailment seam is guarded HERE too,
304	            # so a raising INJECTED entail_fn (or any fault the default seam did not already swallow)
305	            # degrades to None => KEEP, never crashes the prune loop. Log loud, never ``except: pass``.
306	            try:
307	                verdict = efn(span, sentence)  # premise=span, hypothesis=sentence (ALCE/DeepTRACE)
308	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — any seam fault => UNKNOWN => KEEP (fail-open)
309	                logger.warning(
310	                    "[citation_set_minimizer] entail_fn raised on ev_id=%s (%s); "
311	                    "keeping the citation inline (fail-open).", _member_ev_id(m), exc,
312	                )
313	                verdict = None
314	            if verdict is False:
315	                pruned.append(m)  # confident non-entailment => demote to weight channel
316	            else:
317	                survivors.append(m)  # True or None => KEEP inline candidate (fail-open)
318	    else:
319	        survivors = list(members_list)
320	
321	    # ── Leg 2: MVC-demote redundant same-statement corroborators (threshold-tuned). ──────────────
322	    cap = _max_inline() if max_inline is None else max_inline
323	    demoted: list = []
324	    if cap and cap > 0 and survivors:
325	        support = support_of if support_of is not None else (lambda _m: frozenset({_STATEMENT_ATOM}))
326	        cover_idx, redundant_idx = _greedy_set_cover(survivors, support)
327	        # The load-bearing COVER always stays inline (dropping it would leave an atom uncited). Fill
328	        # the remaining inline slots with the highest-weight redundant corroborators up to the cap;
329	        # demote the rest to the weight channel. Effective inline count is max(|cover|, cap).
330	        keep_extra = max(0, cap - len(cover_idx))
331	        redundant_sorted = sorted(
332	            redundant_idx, key=lambda i: _member_weight(survivors[i]), reverse=True
333	        )
334	        kept_idx = set(cover_idx) | set(redundant_sorted[:keep_extra])
335	        inline = [survivors[i] for i in range(len(survivors)) if i in kept_idx]
336	        demoted = [survivors[i] for i in range(len(survivors)) if i not in kept_idx]
337	    else:
338	        inline = survivors  # demotion disabled (default) => prune-only; all survivors stay inline
339	
340	    weight = pruned + demoted
341	    return MinCiteResult(
342	        inline_members=inline,
343	        weight_members=weight,
344	        pruned_members=pruned,
345	        demoted_members=demoted,
346	        enabled=True,
347	    )
348

1	"""Deterministic post-render citation / truncation normalizer.
2	
3	Scope (I-extract-001 Layer-B, the two classes NO extractor or LLM-judge can repair):
4	
5	* **Class 4 - orphan citations.** Bare/leading bracket clusters that carry a
6	  reference but no claim of their own.  In the *real* rendered ``report.md`` they
7	  appear inline as a spurious sentence period wedged between two trailing
8	  citation clusters - ``...across the world.[8].[9][10] The deployment...`` (176
9	  such glues in the banked ``drb_72`` cert report; zero stand-alone bracket
10	  lines).  The ``.[9][10]`` is a render artifact: the references trail the SAME
11	  sentence, so collapsing ``].[`` -> ``][`` merges them onto that sentence and
12	  preserves attribution EXACTLY (no migration, no guess).  Multi-citation per
13	  claim is corroboration, not noise (CLAUDE.md SS-1.3).  The segmented/standalone
14	  forms named in the issue (bare ``[9][10]`` line, leading ``.[19][20] prose``)
15	  are also handled: their clusters migrate to the *immediately adjacent* prose
16	  line, and ONLY then - a blank gap / heading / table breaks ownership, so the
17	  unit is left in place and FLAGGED (never guessed, never deleted).
18	
19	* **Class 3 - mid-word truncation.** A span cut glued with ``.; `` -
20	  ``aggregate statis.; bstitution``, ``employ.; ment``.  The two fragments are
21	  pieces of two DIFFERENT words ("statistics" + "substitution"), so no
22	  deterministic repair exists.  This detector therefore FLAGS only and leaves the
23	  text untouched (CLAUDE.md SS-1.3: when uncertain, leave it + flag).
24	
25	Honest scope: only the ``.; `` glue is in scope here.  The other truncation
26	shapes in the substrate (end-of-text clause cuts, span cuts at the ``[N]``
27	marker boundary such as ``...le.[105]``) need a known-word basis - they live in
28	``scripts/iwire013_sec11_forensic_audit.py`` and the Layer-B LLM judge, NOT in
29	this pure-deterministic module.
30	
31	The faithfulness engine is FROZEN and is not touched: this normalizer runs on the
32	rendered text downstream of it.  Pure deterministic (regex only); no LLM, no
33	network.  Every change is recorded as a :class:`NormalizationFlag`; nothing is
34	silently dropped.
35	"""
36	
37	from __future__ import annotations
38	
39	import logging
40	import re
41	from dataclasses import dataclass
42	
43	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
44	
45	# --- canary -----------------------------------------------------------------
46	CANARY_TAG = "[citation_normalizer]"
47	
48	# --- flag kinds (FLAG, never silently drop; CLAUDE.md SS-1.3) ----------------
49	FLAG_ORPHAN_INLINE_COLLAPSED = "orphan_inline_collapsed"
50	FLAG_ORPHAN_LINE_MIGRATED = "orphan_line_migrated"
51	FLAG_ORPHAN_UNATTACHED = "orphan_unattached"
52	FLAG_TRUNCATION_MIDWORD = "truncation_midword"
53	
54	ACTION_REPAIRED = "repaired"
55	ACTION_FLAGGED = "flagged"
56	
57	# --- pattern constants ------------------------------------------------------
58	# Orphan class 4, PRIMARY (real post-render) form: a citation cluster, a spurious
59	# sentence period, then another citation cluster with NO prose between them.
60	# ``[8].[9]`` -> ``[8][9]``: drops the artifact period, merging the trailing
61	# clusters onto the SAME sentence (attribution unchanged). The pattern is
62	# DIGIT-ANCHORED on both sides (lookbehind ``\d]`` / lookahead ``[\d``) so it only
63	# ever removes the spurious period BETWEEN two numeric citation clusters - a plain
64	# ``].[`` in code / array / JSON prose (``a[3].[index]``) is left untouched. The
65	# match is the lone period; the replacement is empty (delete it).
66	INLINE_ORPHAN_GLUE_PATTERN = re.compile(r"(?<=\d\])\.(?=\[\d)")
67	INLINE_ORPHAN_GLUE_REPLACEMENT = ""
68	
69	# A single bracketed numeric citation token, e.g. ``[12]``.
70	CITATION_TOKEN_PATTERN = re.compile(r"\[\d{1,4}\]")
71	
72	# Orphan class 4, segmented/standalone forms named in I-extract-001:
73	#   bare line     "[9][10]" / ".[19][20]"  (whole line = punctuation + clusters)
74	#   leading prose ".[19][20] The study ..." (line starts punct+clusters, then prose)
75	BARE_ORPHAN_LINE_PATTERN = re.compile(r"^[\s.,;:]*(?:\[\d{1,4}\][\s.,;:]*)+$")
76	LEADING_ORPHAN_PREFIX_PATTERN = re.compile(r"^(\s*[.,;:]\s*(?:\[\d{1,4}\]\s*)+)(\S.*)$")
77	
78	# Optional markdown list marker stripped before inspecting a line body.
79	LIST_MARKER_PATTERN = re.compile(r"^(\s*(?:[-*+]|\d{1,3}[.)])\s+)(.*)$")
80	
81	# Truncation class 3: the ``.; `` mid-word span-cut glue. The pre-glue token must
82	# be at least this long so short abbreviations ("et al.;", "etc.;", "cf.;") do not
83	# false-flag; the cut fragments in the artifact are always long ("statis", "employ").
84	MID_WORD_TRUNCATION_MIN_PREFIX_LEN = 4
85	MID_WORD_TRUNCATION_PATTERN = re.compile(
86	    r"[A-Za-z]{%d,}\.;\s*[a-z]+" % MID_WORD_TRUNCATION_MIN_PREFIX_LEN
87	)
88	
89	# Width of context captured around an inline-glue repair for the audit flag.
90	_GLUE_CONTEXT_BEFORE = 6
91	_GLUE_CONTEXT_AFTER = 8
92	
93	ALPHA_PATTERN = re.compile(r"[A-Za-z]")
94	
95	
96	@dataclass(frozen=True)
97	class NormalizationFlag:
98	    """One auditable normalizer action (repair or flag-for-review)."""
99	
100	    line_index: int
101	    kind: str
102	    offending_span: str
103	    action: str
104	
105	
106	@dataclass
107	class NormalizationResult:
108	    """Outcome of :func:`normalize_citations_and_truncation`."""
109	
110	    text: str
111	    flags: list[NormalizationFlag]
112	    line_count: int
113	    inline_collapsed: int
114	    orphan_migrated: int
115	    orphan_flagged: int
116	    truncation_flagged: int
117	
118	    @property
119	    def changed(self) -> bool:
120	        """True iff any repair altered the text."""
121	        return bool(self.inline_collapsed or self.orphan_migrated)
122	
123	    @property
124	    def canary(self) -> str:
125	        """Behavioral canary with real per-run counts."""
126	        return (
127	            f"{CANARY_TAG} lines={self.line_count} "
128	            f"inline_collapsed={self.inline_collapsed} "
129	            f"orphan_migrated={self.orphan_migrated} "
130	            f"orphan_flagged={self.orphan_flagged} "
131	            f"truncation_flagged={self.truncation_flagged}"
132	        )
133	
134	
135	def _split_list_marker(line: str) -> tuple[str, str]:
136	    """Split a line into its markdown list-marker prefix and the body."""
137	    match = LIST_MARKER_PATTERN.match(line)
138	    if match:
139	        return match.group(1), match.group(2)
140	    return "", line
141	
142	
143	def _is_prose_target(line: str) -> bool:
144	    """True iff ``line`` is real prose a migrated citation may attach to.
145	
146	    Headings, table rows, blockquotes and bracket-only lines are not valid
147	    ownership targets, so a citation is never migrated onto them.
148	    """
149	    stripped = line.strip()
150	    if not stripped:
151	        return False
152	    if stripped[0] in "#|>":
153	        return False
154	    if BARE_ORPHAN_LINE_PATTERN.match(stripped):
155	        return False
156	    return bool(ALPHA_PATTERN.search(stripped))
157	
158	
159	def _adjacent_prose_target(out_lines: list[str]) -> int | None:
160	    """Index of the immediately adjacent prose line, else ``None``.
161	
162	    Only the nearest already-emitted non-blank line qualifies; a blank gap or a
163	    non-prose line (heading/table) breaks claim ownership -> no migration target
164	    (the orphan is then flagged-and-preserved, never guessed).
165	    """
166	    for idx in range(len(out_lines) - 1, -1, -1):
167	        candidate = out_lines[idx]
168	        if not candidate.strip():
169	            return None
170	        return idx if _is_prose_target(candidate) else None
171	    return None
172	
173	
174	def _collapse_inline_glue(
175	    line: str, line_index: int, flags: list[NormalizationFlag]
176	) -> tuple[str, int]:
177	    """Collapse every ``].[`` glue on a line to ``][``; record one flag each."""
178	    collapsed = 0
179	    while True:
180	        match = INLINE_ORPHAN_GLUE_PATTERN.search(line)
181	        if match is None:
182	            break
183	        start = max(0, match.start() - _GLUE_CONTEXT_BEFORE)
184	        end = min(len(line), match.end() + _GLUE_CONTEXT_AFTER)
185	        span = line[start:end]
186	        line = line[: match.start()] + INLINE_ORPHAN_GLUE_REPLACEMENT + line[match.end() :]
187	        collapsed += 1
188	        flags.append(
189	            NormalizationFlag(line_index, FLAG_ORPHAN_INLINE_COLLAPSED, span, ACTION_REPAIRED)
190	        )
191	    return line, collapsed
192	
193	
194	def normalize_citations_and_truncation(text: str) -> NormalizationResult:
195	    """Normalize orphan-citation and mid-word-truncation render artifacts.
196	
197	    Args:
198	        text: the rendered report text (post-render, downstream of the frozen
199	            faithfulness engine).
200	
201	    Returns:
202	        A :class:`NormalizationResult` carrying the normalized ``text``, the
203	        per-action audit ``flags``, and the behavioral counts surfaced in
204	        :attr:`NormalizationResult.canary`.
205	    """
206	    lines = text.split("\n")
207	    flags: list[NormalizationFlag] = []
208	    out_lines: list[str] = []
209	    inline_collapsed = 0
210	    orphan_migrated = 0
211	    orphan_flagged = 0
212	    truncation_flagged = 0
213	
214	    for line_index, raw_line in enumerate(lines):
215	        # Stage 1: faithful same-sentence collapse of the inline ].[ glue.
216	        line, collapsed = _collapse_inline_glue(raw_line, line_index, flags)
217	        inline_collapsed += collapsed
218	
219	        marker_prefix, body = _split_list_marker(line)
220	        body_stripped = body.strip()
221	
222	        # Stage 2a: bare bracket-only line (no prose) -> migrate or flag.
223	        if body_stripped and BARE_ORPHAN_LINE_PATTERN.match(body_stripped) and not ALPHA_PATTERN.search(body_stripped):
224	            clusters = CITATION_TOKEN_PATTERN.findall(body_stripped)
225	            target = _adjacent_prose_target(out_lines)
226	            if target is not None:
227	                out_lines[target] = out_lines[target].rstrip() + "".join(clusters)
228	                flags.append(
229	                    NormalizationFlag(line_index, FLAG_ORPHAN_LINE_MIGRATED, body_stripped, ACTION_REPAIRED)
230	                )
231	                orphan_migrated += 1
232	                continue  # line collapsed away (its citations migrated)
233	            flags.append(
234	                NormalizationFlag(line_index, FLAG_ORPHAN_UNATTACHED, body_stripped, ACTION_FLAGGED)
235	            )
236	            orphan_flagged += 1
237	            out_lines.append(line)
238	            continue
239	
240	        # Stage 2b: leading orphan prefix on otherwise-real prose -> migrate or flag.
241	        leading = LEADING_ORPHAN_PREFIX_PATTERN.match(body)
242	        if leading is not None and ALPHA_PATTERN.search(leading.group(2)):
243	            prefix, rest = leading.group(1), leading.group(2)
244	            clusters = CITATION_TOKEN_PATTERN.findall(prefix)
245	            target = _adjacent_prose_target(out_lines)
246	            if target is not None:
247	                out_lines[target] = out_lines[target].rstrip() + "".join(clusters)
248	                repaired_line = marker_prefix + rest
249	                flags.append(
250	                    NormalizationFlag(line_index, FLAG_ORPHAN_LINE_MIGRATED, prefix.strip(), ACTION_REPAIRED)
251	                )
252	                orphan_migrated += 1
253	                out_lines.append(repaired_line)
254	                truncation_flagged += _flag_truncation(repaired_line, line_index, flags)
255	                continue
256	            flags.append(
257	                NormalizationFlag(line_index, FLAG_ORPHAN_UNATTACHED, prefix.strip(), ACTION_FLAGGED)
258	            )
259	            orphan_flagged += 1
260	            out_lines.append(line)
261	            truncation_flagged += _flag_truncation(line, line_index, flags)
262	            continue
263	
264	        # Stage 3: mid-word truncation -> flag only, text unchanged.
265	        out_lines.append(line)
266	        truncation_flagged += _flag_truncation(line, line_index, flags)
267	
268	    result = NormalizationResult(
269	        text="\n".join(out_lines),
270	        flags=flags,
271	        line_count=len(lines),
272	        inline_collapsed=inline_collapsed,
273	        orphan_migrated=orphan_migrated,
274	        orphan_flagged=orphan_flagged,
275	        truncation_flagged=truncation_flagged,
276	    )
277	    logger.info(result.canary)
278	    return result
279	
280	
281	def _flag_truncation(line: str, line_index: int, flags: list[NormalizationFlag]) -> int:
282	    """Record a flag for every mid-word ``.; `` truncation span on a line."""
283	    count = 0
284	    for match in MID_WORD_TRUNCATION_PATTERN.finditer(line):
285	        flags.append(
286	            NormalizationFlag(line_index, FLAG_TRUNCATION_MIDWORD, match.group(0), ACTION_FLAGGED)
287	        )
288	        count += 1
289	    return count

1	"""I-gen-005 atom-first architecture: extract STRUCTURED claim atoms
2	from evidence direct_quotes.
3	
4	Per Codex strategy verdict 2026-05-26
5	(`codex_quality_strategy_verdict.txt`) recommended_path #2 + design
6	verdict APPROVE_DESIGN + diff iter-1 REQUEST_CHANGES (4 P1s closed
7	this iteration).
8	
9	Why this module exists:
10	    The current architecture lets V4 Pro write open prose, then catches
11	    fabrications post-hoc via strict_verify. Codex's read: that's
12	    backwards. The fix is to PRE-EXTRACT verifiable facts (atoms) from
13	    cited evidence spans BEFORE generation, then constrain V4 Pro to
14	    cite atom_ids that map to fixed spans. V4 Pro becomes the
15	    rhetorical/synthesis layer, not the fact source.
16	
17	Iter 2 (Codex iter-1 REQUEST_CHANGES) closes 4 P1 bugs:
18	    P1.1 unit_extraction_corrupts_units (line 209)
19	        Fix: special-case `%` without word boundary; word units need
20	        leading boundary; restrict fallback unit-scan to immediate
21	        post-value or comma-coordinate context only.
22	
23	    P1.2 numeric_anchor_filter_emits_non_claim_values (line 438)
24	        Fix: NumberRole classifier (OUTCOME / DOSE / TIMEPOINT /
25	        SAMPLE_SIZE / CI_BOUND / CI_LEVEL) runs BEFORE atom creation.
26	        Only OUTCOME numbers become atoms. Doses, timepoints, sample
27	        sizes are stored as metadata fields on the OUTCOME atom they
28	        modify, not as separate atoms.
29	
30	    P1.3 entity_comparator_binding_flips_arms (line 362)
31	        Fix: arm-local entity binding. For each value, find the
32	        nearest arm phrase ("with X", "X mg", "X group") within
33	        ~50 chars to the right (where clinical text typically places
34	        the arm label). Comparator from explicit "versus X" /
35	        "compared to Y" syntax separately.
36	
37	    P1.4 literal_span_expansion_splits_decimals (line 483)
38	        Fix: decimal-aware sentence boundary regex. A period followed
39	        by a digit is part of a decimal (8.21), not a sentence end.
40	        Use ``\\.(?=\\s|$|\\n|[A-Z])`` boundary.
41	
42	Also addresses Codex iter-1 P2s:
43	    - primary_section field added; section_tags computed dynamically
44	      from extracted comparator + dose + safety presence
45	    - Endpoint vocabulary extended with renal, CV/HF, obesity,
46	      metabolic, safety subgroup, and statistical-metadata terms
47	
48	Module remains INTENTIONALLY pure-Python regex — no LLM call. Fast,
49	deterministic, reproducible. Atoms come from actual evidence text;
50	nothing fabricated here.
51	"""
52	
53	from __future__ import annotations
54	
55	import re
56	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
57	from enum import Enum
58	from typing import Any, Optional
59	
60	
61	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
62	# ClaimAtom — the structured record V4 Pro will cite from
63	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
64	
65	@dataclass(frozen=True)
66	class ClaimAtom:
67	    """A single verifiable claim extracted from an evidence span.
68	
69	    Frozen because atom_id resolution depends on stable identity through
70	    the pipeline. 14 fields (13 from Codex APPROVE_DESIGN + primary_section
71	    added per Codex iter-1 diff review P2).
72	    """
73	
74	    # Identity + provenance:
75	    atom_id: str            # "atom_001"
76	    evidence_id: str        # "ev_017"
77	    span_start: int         # offset in direct_quote where the atom is grounded
78	    span_end: int           # offset in direct_quote (exclusive)
79	    literal_text: str       # verbatim text from the span supporting this claim
80	
81	    # Semantic fields:
82	    entity: str             # "tirzepatide 15 mg" / "SURPASS-2" / "apixaban"
83	    endpoint: str           # "HbA1c" / "body weight" / "stroke"
84	    comparator: str         # "semaglutide" / "placebo" / "" if none
85	    timepoint: str          # "40 weeks" / "1.8 years" / "" if none
86	    value: str              # "-2.30" / "0.69" / "82-86"
87	    unit: str               # "percentage points" / "kg" / "%" / "RR" / ""
88	
89	    # Routing:
90	    primary_section: str            # Single best-fit section
91	    section_tags: tuple[str, ...]   # All sections this atom can serve
92	    tier: str                       # "T1" / "T2" / ...
93	
94	    # Codex APPROVE_DESIGN additions:
95	    value_signed: bool              # True if the value is negative
96	    confidence: str                 # "high" | "medium" | "low"
97	    provenance_class: str           # "abstract_only" | "open_access" | ...
98	    source_paper_title: str         # for refusal disclosure
99	
100	
101	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
102	# NumberRole — what role does a numeric anchor play in clinical text?
103	# Codex iter-1 P1 #2 fix: classify each number BEFORE atom creation so
104	# timepoints / doses / sample-sizes / CI bounds don't pollute the catalog.
105	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
106	
107	class NumberRole(Enum):
108	    OUTCOME = "outcome"          # Primary or secondary clinical endpoint value
109	    DOSE = "dose"                # Drug dose (5 mg, 0.5 μg)
110	    TIMEPOINT = "timepoint"      # Follow-up duration (40 weeks, 1.8 years)
111	    SAMPLE_SIZE = "sample_size"  # Number of participants (N=1879)
112	    CI_BOUND = "ci_bound"        # Confidence interval limits (0.58 to 0.95)
113	    CI_LEVEL = "ci_level"        # CI confidence level (95% CI)
114	    P_VALUE = "p_value"          # P-value (P<0.001)
115	    UNKNOWN = "unknown"          # Couldn't classify
116	
117	
118	# Classifiers — small per-role regex patterns evaluated against the
119	# IMMEDIATE neighborhood of the number (±20 chars). Order matters: first
120	# match wins. OUTCOME is the implicit default if no classifier hits.
121	
122	# Dose: number immediately followed by mg/μg/U/etc with a drug name nearby
123	_DOSE_UNIT_RE = re.compile(
124	    r"^\s?(mg|μg|mcg|g|IU|U)\b",
125	    re.IGNORECASE,
126	)
127	
128	# Timepoint: surrounded by "at/after/by/over" + "weeks/months/years/..."
129	_TIMEPOINT_BEFORE_RE = re.compile(
130	    r"\b(?:at|after|by|over|in|through|for|of)\s+\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s*"
131	    r"(?:weeks?|months?|years?|days?|hours?|min(?:utes?)?)\b$",
132	    re.IGNORECASE,
133	)
134	_TIMEPOINT_TRAILING_RE = re.compile(
135	    r"^\s?(?:weeks?|months?|years?|days?|hours?|min(?:utes?)?)\b",
136	    re.IGNORECASE,
137	)
138	
139	# Sample size: number preceded by N=, n=, "enrolled", "randomized",
140	# "patients with", "participants with", "trial of", etc.
141	_SAMPLE_SIZE_LEFT_RE = re.compile(
142	    r"(?:\b(?:[Nn]\s*=\s*|enrolled|randomi[sz]ed|"
143	    r"included|recruited|trial\s+of|patients\s+with|"
144	    r"participants\s+with|adults\s+with|subjects\s+with|"
145	    r"a\s+total\s+of|cohort\s+of|study\s+of|population\s+of)\s*)$",
146	    re.IGNORECASE,
147	)
148	# Sample size: number followed by "patients", "participants", "adults",
149	# "subjects" (e.g., "1879 adults", "18,201 patients").
150	_SAMPLE_SIZE_RIGHT_RE = re.compile(
151	    r"^\s*"
152	    r"(?:patients?|participants?|adults?|subjects?|"
153	    r"individuals|persons|men|women)\b",
154	    re.IGNORECASE,
155	)
156	
157	# CI bound: number is inside parens with "CI" pattern nearby. Iter-3
158	# replaced the iter-2 brittle left/right regex chain with the
159	# _is_inside_ci_parens() structural walk; iter-4 added
160	# _is_ci_bound_unparen() for non-parenthesized "HR 0.74, 95% CI 0.58,
161	# 0.95" forms. Only _CI_PAREN_LEFT_RE remains as a defense-in-depth
162	# catch for paren-only ranges without the "CI" word literal.
163	_CI_PAREN_LEFT_RE = re.compile(r"[\(\[][-−]?\d+(?:\.\d+)?\s*(?:to|[-–—])\s*$")
164	
165	# CI level: number directly followed by "% CI" or "% confidence interval"
166	_CI_LEVEL_RIGHT_RE = re.compile(
167	    r"^\s?%?\s*(?:CI|confidence\s+interval)\b",
168	    re.IGNORECASE,
169	)
170	
171	# P-value: "P<", "P=", "P>", "p value", "P-value"
172	_P_VALUE_LEFT_RE = re.compile(
173	    r"\b[Pp]\s*[<>=]\s*$|\b[Pp][-\s]?(?:value)?\s*=?\s*$",
174	)
175	
176	
177	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
178	# Endpoint vocabulary (expanded per Codex iter-1 P1 review)
179	# Each tuple = (regex_pattern, canonical_name, primary_section, secondary_tags)
180	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

256	    (re.compile(r"\b(?:treatment[-\s]emergent\s+)?adverse\s+events?\b|\bTEAE\b", re.IGNORECASE),

258	    (re.compile(r"\b(?:discontinuation|discontinued|treatment\s+withdrawal)\b", re.IGNORECASE),
259	     "discontinuation", "Safety", ("Safety",)),

281	     "gallbladder disease", "Safety", ("Safety",)),

283	    # ── Treatment differences (efficacy + comparative) ──
284	    (re.compile(r"\bestimated\s+treatment\s+difference|\bETD\b", re.IGNORECASE),
285	     "estimated treatment difference", "Comparative", ("Efficacy", "Comparative")),
286	    (re.compile(r"\btreatment\s+difference\b", re.IGNORECASE),

292	    (re.compile(r"\bbioavailability\b", re.IGNORECASE),
293	     "bioavailability", "Mechanism", ("Mechanism",)),

371	# I-gen-005 Step 3k (Codex APPROVE_DESIGN iter-1): markdown safety-table
372	# row detection + per-cell atom extraction. Target: safety tables with
373	# format like:
374	#   | Nausea | 82 (17.4) | 111 | 90 (19.2) | 124 | 104 (22.1) | ... |
375	# Each cell with a percentage in parens or trailing % becomes a per-cell
376	# atom with endpoint = row header. Column-to-arm mapping is DEFERRED
377	# (Codex P2: trial-name entity fallback acceptable for now).
378	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
379	
380	# Safety endpoint vocab for table-row headers. Mirrors the safety subset
381	# of _ENDPOINT_VOCAB so any row labeled with a known safety endpoint
382	# becomes a per-cell extraction candidate.
383	_SAFETY_TABLE_HEADER_RE = re.compile(
384	    r"(?:"
385	    r"^|^\W+|"  # row prefix tolerant (markdown bullets, leading symbols)
386	    r"\b"
387	    r")"
388	    r"(?P<endpoint>"
389	    r"nausea|vomiting|diarrh(?:o)?ea|constipation|abdominal\s+pain|"
390	    r"dyspepsia|decreased\s+appetite|"
391	    r"hypoglycem(?:ia|ic)|"
392	    r"pancreatitis|gallbladder(?:\s+disease)?|cholelithiasis|cholecystitis|"
393	    r"retinopathy|"
394	    r"injection[-\s]?site\s+reactions?|"
395	    r"(?:all\s+)?(?:treatment[-\s]?related\s+|treatment[-\s]?emergent\s+)?"
396	    r"adverse\s+events?(?:\s+leading\s+to\s+(?:treatment\s+)?discontinuation"
397	    r"(?:\s+of\s+\w+(?:\s+(?:or|and)\s+\w+)?)?)?|"
398	    r"TEAE|"
399	    r"serious\s+adverse\s+events?|SAEs?|"
400	    r"discontinuation|treatment\s+withdrawal|"
401	    r"patients\s+with\s+(?:≥|>=|≧)\s*1\s+(?:serious\s+)?adverse\s+events?|"
402	    r"patients\s+with\s+at\s+least\s+1\s+(?:serious\s+)?adverse\s+events?|"
403	    r"(?:all\s+)?gastrointestinal\s+(?:adverse\s+)?events?|"
404	    r"(?:all\s+)?GI\s+(?:adverse\s+)?events?|"
405	    r"thyroid\s+(?:carcinoma|cancer|c[-\s]?cell)|MTC|"
406	    r"C[-\s]?cell\s+(?:hyperplasia|cancer)|"
407	    r"GI\s+bleeding|gastrointestinal\s+bleeding|major\s+bleeding|"
408	    r"intracranial\s+hemorrhage|ICH|"
409	    # Codex Step 3k diff iter-1 P2 #2: expanded vocab for fresh tables
410	    r"death|all[-\s]?cause\s+mortality|"
411	    r"fatigue|asthenia|headache|dizziness|"
412	    r"hypersensitivity|allergic\s+reactions?|anaphylaxis|"
413	    r"acute\s+kidney\s+injury|AKI|renal\s+(?:impairment|failure)|"
414	    r"neoplasm|cancer|malignancy|"
415	    r"calcitonin|"
416	    r"hyperglycem(?:ia|ic)|"
417	    r"infection|"
418	    r"urinary\s+tract\s+infection|UTI|"
419	    r"upper\s+respiratory\s+tract\s+infection|URTI|"
420	    r"nasopharyngitis"
421	    r")",
422	    re.IGNORECASE,
423	)
