1	"""V30 Phase-2 M-63 — contract-driven section runner.
2	
3	Replaces legacy per-section LLM prompts with M-58 slot-bound
4	prose for entities that have a FrameRow. Returns the same
5	`SectionResult` shape as legacy `_run_section` so downstream
6	assembly is unchanged.
7	
8	## Architecture (per Phase-2 plan M-63 Fix #1)
9	
10	- SECTION-level SectionResult, NOT slot-level. Each contract
11	  section (Efficacy, Mechanism, Regulatory for clinical)
12	  becomes ONE SectionResult whose verified_text concatenates
13	  `### {subsection_title}` headings + M-58 body prose per
14	  slot.
15	- Multi-entity slots render N blocks, each with its own
16	  `[bound_ev_id]` citation.
17	- Gap rows skip the LLM via `compose_gap_payload`.
18	- Non-gap rows call `build_slot_fill_prompt` → LLM → JSON →
19	  `parse_slot_fill_response` (raises on fabrication).
20	- Contract entity ids are registered into `evidence_pool` keyed
21	  by entity_id so the generalized citation-rewrite regex
22	  (M-63 Fix #3) resolves `[surpass_2_primary]` markers.
23	
24	## Dispatch
25	
26	`ContractSectionPlanExt` subclasses `SectionPlan`. When the
27	legacy orchestration loop sees an instance, it calls
28	`_run_contract_section(plan, ...)` instead of the legacy
29	`_run_section`. This is how `generate_multi_section_report`
30	routes contract vs legacy prose.
31	
32	## No legacy overlap
33	
34	- M-41c (under-framed trial sentences) is no-op by construction:
35	  body sentences carry `field: value [id].` format, no trial
36	  short-names. M-41c keys on short-names.
37	- M-44 (primary-citation validator) passes trivially: every
38	  sentence cites the bound ev_id.
39	- M-50 per-trial subsection generator is SKIPPED for entities
40	  whose anchor maps to a contract slot (integration layer
41	  enforces this).
42	"""
43	from __future__ import annotations
44	
45	import asyncio
46	import logging
47	import os
48	import re
49	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
50	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any
51	
52	# I-run11-010 (#1056, S2): minimum direct_quote length (chars) for a frame row to carry a verifiable
53	# citation span. A METADATA_ONLY row below this is routed to gap disclosure (LAW VI: env-overridable).
54	_MIN_VERIFIABLE_SPAN_CHARS = int(os.getenv("PG_MIN_VERIFIABLE_SPAN_CHARS", "50"))
55	
56	from ..nodes.contract_outline import ContractSectionPlan, ContractSlotPlan
57	from ..nodes.report_contract import RequiredEntity
58	from ..retrieval.frame_fetcher import FrameRow, ProvenanceClass
59	from .slot_fill import (
60	    SlotFillParseError,
61	    SlotFillPayload,
62	    build_slot_fill_prompt,
63	    build_slot_narrative_prompt,
64	    compose_gap_payload,
65	    parse_slot_fill_response,
66	    render_slot_prose,
67	)
68	
69	if TYPE_CHECKING:
70	    from .multi_section_generator import SectionPlan, SectionResult
71	
72	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.contract_section_runner")
73	
74	
75	# I-faith-001 Fix A: failure_reasons prefixes that mark a NUMERIC drop.
76	# A sentence dropped for one of these reasons is a numeric fabrication
77	# (a number/integer not present in the cited span) and must NEVER be
78	# laundered back by the M-69 Fix #4 contract-entity rescue. The rescue
79	# exists only to undo content-overlap false-drops on verbatim M-58 slot
80	# prose. `failure_reasons` strings are "reason:entity[:detail]" — Fix D
81	# appends a ":missing=[...]" detail to the integer reason, so the prefix
82	# is extracted via split(":", 1)[0] to stay match-safe.
83	_NUMERIC_DROP_PREFIXES: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
84	    "number_not_in_any_cited_span",
85	    "no_integer_overlap_any_cited_span",
86	})
87	
88	# I-faith-001 Fix A refinement (Codex gate P1): a deterministic gap-disclosure
89	# ("<field>: not extractable from available primary content") is an HONEST
90	# disclosure, never a numeric CLAIM. A digit embedded in the field LABEL
91	# (e.g. "Baseline HbA1c", "COVID-19", "T2 diabetes") must NOT make it look
92	# like a numeric fabrication and strip its rescue eligibility -> the honest
93	# disclosure would vanish from a partially-rendered slot. Such disclosures
94	# are always rescue-eligible. (Real narrative numeric fabrications are
95	# already rescue-INELIGIBLE via Fix B's allow_rescue=False narrative stream,
96	# independent of this guard.)
97	_GAP_DISCLOSURE_MARKER = "not extractable from available primary content"
98	
99	# I-deepfix-001 Wave-3 PART 2 ARM B (#1344): the DEGRADED-VERIFY honest disclosure
100	# (``verified_compose._degraded_verify_disclosure`` — a judge-outage-emptied basket)
101	# is ALSO a disclosure placeholder, NOT substantive verified prose. This sentinel
102	# phrase is produced ONLY by that ARM-B-ON path, so recognizing it is inert when
103	# PG_DEGRADED_VERIFY_DISCLOSURE is OFF (no text ever carries it). Recognizing it keeps
104	# the frame_coverage honesty override from mis-scoring the disclosure as substantive
105	# prose or as a numeric fabrication (the label carries a count digit).
106	_DEGRADED_VERIFY_DISCLOSURE_MARKER = "entailment verification was unavailable this run"
107	
108	
109	def _is_gap_disclosure_sentence(text: Any) -> bool:
110	    """Shared predicate (single source of truth): True iff a sentence is a
111	    disclosure placeholder rather than SUBSTANTIVE verified prose — either the
112	    deterministic gap-disclosure ("<field>: not extractable from available primary
113	    content") OR the I-deepfix-001 Wave-3 ARM B degraded-verify disclosure
114	    ("verification incomplete: ... entailment verification was unavailable this run").
115	
116	    I-ready-017 FX-07b leg-2 (#1111, root-cause design P2): centralizing this on
117	    `_GAP_DISCLOSURE_MARKER` so the marker cannot drift and silently let a
118	    placeholder count as substantive prose in the frame_coverage honesty
119	    override (the Class-B placeholder-kept escape).
120	    """
121	    lowered = str(text or "").lower()
122	    return (
123	        _GAP_DISCLOSURE_MARKER in lowered
124	        or _DEGRADED_VERIFY_DISCLOSURE_MARKER in lowered
125	    )
126	
127	
128	def _drop_is_numeric(sv: Any) -> bool:
129	    """True iff a dropped SentenceVerification failed for a NUMERIC reason
130	    AND is not a deterministic gap-disclosure.
131	
132	    Inspects the `failure_reasons` LIST (there is no scalar drop_reason)
133	    and returns True if ANY reason's prefix (the text before the first
134	    ':') is a numeric-failure prefix. Used by the M-69 rescue loop to
135	    exclude numeric fabrications from rescue (I-faith-001 Fix A). Gap-
136	    disclosure sentences are exempt (Codex P1): an embedded label digit
137	    must not block their rescue.
138	    """
139	    sentence = str(getattr(sv, "sentence", "") or "")
140	    if _is_gap_disclosure_sentence(sentence):
141	        return False
142	    failure_reasons = getattr(sv, "failure_reasons", None) or []
143	    return any(
144	        str(reason).split(":", 1)[0] in _NUMERIC_DROP_PREFIXES
145	        for reason in failure_reasons
146	    )
147	
148	
149	def _verify_one_stream(
150	    *,
151	    raw_draft: str,
152	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
153	    contract_entity_ids: set[str],
154	    rewrite_fn: Any,
155	    strict_verify_fn: Any,
156	    allow_rescue: bool,
157	    stream_label: str,
158	) -> tuple[list[Any], list[Any], list[Any], int, str]:
159	    """Rewrite + strict-verify ONE provenance stream, optionally applying
160	    the M-69 contract-entity rescue (I-faith-001 Fix B — stream separation).
161	
162	    The DETERMINISTIC stream (M-58 / M-70 verbatim-guarded slot prose) is
163	    rescue-ELIGIBLE: ``allow_rescue=True`` runs the M-69 Fix #4 rescue with
164	    the Fix A `_drop_is_numeric` guard, restoring content-overlap false-drops
165	    while never laundering numeric fabrications.
166	
167	    The NARRATIVE stream (free-form `build_slot_narrative_prompt` LLM
168	    paragraph) is rescue-INELIGIBLE: ``allow_rescue=False`` keeps EVERY
169	    dropped sentence dropped, so a narrative sentence that fails
170	    `verify_sentence_provenance` is never restored. This is the structural
171	    fix that closes the run-9 leak: the rescue blanket no longer covers the
172	    fabrication-prone stream.
173	
174	    Returns:
175	        kept_sentences:   verified sentences (+ rescued, if allow_rescue)
176	        rescued:          the rescued SVs (empty when allow_rescue is False)
177	        dropped_final:    dropped SVs AFTER removing any rescued ones
178	        total_in:         report.total_in (for the kept/dropped math)
179	        rewritten_draft:  the span-token-rewritten draft for this stream
180	
181	    An empty `raw_draft` short-circuits to all-empty results (no rewrite /
182	    verify call), which is the identical no-op behavior the disabled /
183	    no-narrative case produced pre-Fix-B.
184	    """
185	    if not raw_draft:
186	        return [], [], [], 0, ""
187	
188	    # Rewrite citation markers to span tokens (M-63 Fix #3 generalized
189	    # regex picks up contract entity ids registered into evidence_pool by
190	    # the integration layer).
191	    rewritten_draft, _converted, _unverifiable = rewrite_fn(
192	        raw_draft, evidence_pool,
193	    )
194	
195	    # Strict verify — every deterministic sentence is `Field: value [#ev:...]`
196	    # and the span comes from FrameRow.direct_quote.
197	    report = strict_verify_fn(rewritten_draft, evidence_pool)
198	    kept_sentences = list(getattr(report, "kept_sentences", []) or [])
199	    dropped_sentences = list(getattr(report, "dropped_sentences", []) or [])
200	    total_in = int(getattr(report, "total_in", 0) or 0)
201	
202	    rescued: list[Any] = []
203	    if allow_rescue:
204	        # M-69 Fix #4 rescue (deterministic stream only). Restore a dropped
205	        # sentence iff its primary token resolves to a contract entity_id
206	        # AND it did NOT drop for a numeric reason (Fix A). The legitimate
207	        # content-overlap "not extractable" gap-disclosures (drop for
208	        # no_content_word_overlap, not numeric) remain rescue-eligible.
209	        for sv in dropped_sentences:
210	            toks = getattr(sv, "tokens", None) or []
211	            if not (toks and toks[0].evidence_id in contract_entity_ids):
212	                continue
213	            if _drop_is_numeric(sv):
214	                # Numeric fabrication — never rescue.
215	                continue
216	            rescued.append(sv)
217	        if rescued:
218	            logger.info(
219	                "[m63] M-69 Fix #4 (%s stream): rescued %d "
220	                "strict_verify-dropped contract sentences "
221	                "(M-58 already verified verbatim)",
222	                stream_label, len(rescued),
223	            )
224	            kept_sentences.extend(rescued)
225	
226	    rescued_ids = {id(sv) for sv in rescued}
227	    dropped_final = [
228	        sv for sv in dropped_sentences if id(sv) not in rescued_ids
229	    ]
230	    return kept_sentences, rescued, dropped_final, total_in, rewritten_draft
231	
232	
233	@dataclass
234	class ContractSectionPlanExt:
235	    """Extended section plan carrying the contract info needed
236	    to dispatch to `_run_contract_section`. Mirrors
237	    `SectionPlan`'s public fields so existing orchestration
238	    code treats it as a drop-in via duck-typing.
239	
240	    Codex pass-1 Q2 answer: dedicated dispatch type, not a
241	    sentinel field on legacy `SectionPlan`. This preserves
242	    contract invariants (slots, frame_rows_by_entity,
243	    contract_entities_by_id) explicitly.
244	
245	    Named distinct from M-57's `ContractSectionPlan` per Codex
246	    pass-2 new issue #3.
247	    """
248	
249	    title: str                              # SectionPlan field
250	    focus: str                              # SectionPlan field
251	    ev_ids: list[str]                       # SectionPlan field
252	    # M-63 additions:
253	    slots: tuple[ContractSlotPlan, ...]
254	    frame_rows_by_entity: dict[str, FrameRow]
255	    contract_entities_by_id: dict[str, RequiredEntity]
256	    research_question: str
257	
258	
259	def register_frame_rows_into_evidence_pool(
260	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
261	    frame_rows: tuple[FrameRow, ...],
262	) -> None:
263	    """Register each FrameRow into `evidence_pool` keyed by
264	    entity_id so the generalized citation-rewrite regex
265	    (M-63 Fix #3) can resolve `[entity_id]` markers via
266	    `evidence_pool.get(entity_id)`.
267	
268	    In-place mutation. Each synthesized evidence_pool entry carries:
269	      - evidence_id: entity_id (what the rewriter expects)
270	      - direct_quote: FrameRow.direct_quote (what strict_verify
271	        compares against)
272	      - url: FrameRow.oa_pdf_url or FrameRow.url (for citation
273	        resolution)
274	      - title, authors, journal, year: FrameRow metadata
275	      - v30_frame_row: True (marker for this path)
276	
277	    Codex M-63 REJECT Medium 3 namespace-collision guard:
278	
279	    Contract entity ids are validated at M-54 load time to NOT
280	    match the live-retrieval `^ev_\\d+$` pattern. That's the first
281	    line of defense. This registration path adds defense-in-depth:
282	    if the incoming evidence_pool already has a row at this key
283	    AND that row is NOT a v30_frame_row (i.e., it's a live-
284	    retrieval or legacy-pipeline row), raise rather than clobber.
285	    A loud error here beats a silent generator misattribution.
286	    Pre-existing v30_frame_row entries (e.g., from M-61 curator
287	    completions) may be overwritten — that's the intended
288	    curator-supplied wins semantics.
289	    """
290	    for row in frame_rows:
291	        existing = evidence_pool.get(row.entity_id)
292	        if existing is not None and not existing.get("v30_frame_row"):
293	            raise ValueError(
294	                f"evidence_pool collision at entity_id={row.entity_id!r}: "
295	                f"a non-v30 pool row already occupies this key. Contract "
296	                f"entity ids must not collide with live-retrieval keys. "
297	                f"M-54 schema validation should have prevented this; "
298	                f"loader may be out of sync."
299	            )
300	        evidence_pool[row.entity_id] = {
301	            "evidence_id": row.entity_id,
302	            "direct_quote": row.direct_quote or "",
303	            "url": row.oa_pdf_url or row.url or "",
304	            "title": row.title or "",
305	            "authors": list(row.authors),
306	            "journal": row.journal or "",
307	            "year": row.year,
308	            "doi": row.doi or "",
309	            "pmid": row.pmid or "",
310	            "provenance_class": row.provenance_class.value,
311	            # V30 phase-2 marker
312	            "v30_frame_row": True,
313	            "v30_entity_id": row.entity_id,
314	        }
315	
316	
317	def _build_not_extractable_payload(
318	    slot: ContractSlotPlan,
319	    entity_id: str,
320	    frame_row: FrameRow,
321	    required_fields: tuple[str, ...],
322	) -> SlotFillPayload:
323	    """Build an all-not_extractable SlotFillPayload for a non-gap
324	    row where the LLM failed (network error, schema parse error,
325	    etc.). The payload still cites the bound ev_id so M-59 surfaces
326	    it as FAIL_MIN_FIELDS (curator-actionable) rather than silently
327	    dropping the entity.
328	
329	    Cannot use `compose_gap_payload` — that helper hard-raises on
330	    non-gap provenance by design (Codex M-58 audit symmetric guard
331	    against routing bugs).
332	    """
333	    from .slot_fill import SlotFieldFill, SlotFillPayload as _SFP
334	    fills = tuple(
335	        SlotFieldFill(
336	            field_name=fname,
337	            status="not_extractable",
338	            value=None,
339	            bound_ev_id=entity_id,
340	            source_span=None,
341	        )
342	        for fname in required_fields
343	    )
344	    return _SFP(
345	        slot_id=slot.slot_id,
346	        entity_id=entity_id,
347	        subsection_title=slot.subsection_title,
348	        bound_ev_id=entity_id,
349	        fields=fills,
350	        provenance_class=frame_row.provenance_class.value,
351	    )
352	
353	
354	# A1 (iarch006 RC1) — basket fallback. Default ON; byte-identical when no
355	# basket data is threaded (credibility_analysis None => empty index => no
356	# corroborators). LAW VI: env-overridable kill switch.
357	_A1_BASKET_FALLBACK_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"0", "false", "off", "no", ""})
358	
359	
360	def _a1_basket_fallback_enabled() -> bool:
361	    """A1 — True (default) unless PG_A1_BASKET_FALLBACK is set off. Accepts the
362	    SAME falsey vocabulary as the nearby feature flags (Codex iarch007 P1 #3:

374	    if frame_row.provenance_class == ProvenanceClass.FRAME_GAP_UNRECOVERABLE:

381	# strict_verify kept ZERO composed sentences for a slot. But the slot's BOUND

403	    """FIX 4 — True (default) unless ``PG_CONTRACT_FALSE_GAP_KSPAN`` is set off.
404	    Default ON (activated in the I-deepfix-001 wire+activate wave); accepts the

413	def _kspan_fallback_body(
414	    *,
415	    primary_ev: str,
416	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
417	    marker_num: int,
418	    rewrite_fn: Any,
419	    strict_verify_fn: Any,
420	) -> str | None:
421	    """FIX 4 — if the bound entity has ANY strict_verify-passing span, return a
422	    body reconstructed from ONLY those passing sentences (markers stripped +
423	    leading page chrome excluded, single ``[marker_num]`` re-appended) to render
424	    in place of a false gap; else ``None`` (genuinely no usable span → the caller
425	    gap-discloses).
426	
427	    Same grounded-by-construction idiom as ``abstractive_writer``'s K-span: the
428	    span is fetched primary-source text, so a verbatim restatement carries every
429	    decimal and full content-word overlap and passes strict_verify. We still run
430	    the entity's span through the IDENTICAL rewrite → strict_verify path the
431	    composed body uses (``allow_rescue=False`` — the span must pass on its own
432	    merits, no rescue laundering), so a non-usable span (empty / metadata-only /
433	    numeric-only shell) correctly yields ``None``. The faithfulness engine is
434	    BYTE-UNTOUCHED — this only RE-USES it as the gate."""
435	    row = evidence_pool.get(primary_ev) or {}
436	    span = str(row.get("direct_quote") or "").strip()
437	    if len(span) < _MIN_VERIFIABLE_SPAN_CHARS:
438	        return None
439	    # Build the verify draft by attaching the bound-entity marker to EACH
440	    # sentence of the span (inserted BEFORE the terminal punctuation so the
441	    # marker stays inside its sentence — a marker AFTER the period splits off
442	    # as a token-less fragment and every sentence would false-drop as
443	    # ``no_provenance_token``). Run it through the SAME rewrite→strict_verify
444	    # machinery the composed body uses; ``kept`` non-empty means the bound
445	    # entity has AT LEAST ONE strict_verify-passing span.
446	    from .provenance_generator import split_into_sentences
447	    _marked: list[str] = []
448	    for _sent in split_into_sentences(span):
449	        _sent = _sent.rstrip()
450	        if not _sent:
451	            continue
452	        if _sent[-1] in ".!?":
453	            _marked.append(f"{_sent[:-1].rstrip()} [{primary_ev}]{_sent[-1]}")
454	        else:
455	            _marked.append(f"{_sent} [{primary_ev}]")
456	    if not _marked:
457	        return None
458	    draft = " ".join(_marked)
459	    kept, _rescued, _dropped, _total, _rewritten = _verify_one_stream(
460	        raw_draft=draft,
461	        evidence_pool=evidence_pool,
462	        contract_entity_ids=set(),
463	        rewrite_fn=rewrite_fn,
464	        strict_verify_fn=strict_verify_fn,
465	        allow_rescue=False,
466	        stream_label="fix4_false_gap_kspan",
467	    )
468	    if not kept:
469	        return None
470	    # I-deepfix-001 (#1344) FIX 4 harden: reconstruct the body from ONLY the
471	    # strict_verify-PASSING sentences' cleaned text — NEVER the raw full span,
472	    # which dumps the leading page chrome (ToC / nav bullets / masthead) that
473	    # precedes the real prose. Each rendered sentence individually passed
474	    # strict_verify, so grounding is preserved; the leading chrome (the one
475	    # legit hard-drop per §-1.3) is excluded. Return None only when zero
476	    # sentences leave usable prose after cleaning.
477	    import re as _re  # noqa: PLC0415 (lazy: zero cost off this fallback path)
478	    from .chrome_furniture_screen import _alpha_word_count
479	    # I-deepfix-001 (#1369) FIX A — strip ONLY the EXACT provenance / citation
480	    # marker LITERALS, never a generic lowercase-word pattern. The prior
481	    # `\[[a-z0-9_]+\]` alternative removed ANY single lowercase_snake bracket
482	    # token, so a substantive verified hedge that is a plain lowercase word —
483	    # [unadjusted], [baseline], [placebo], [crude], [sic], [not adjusted] —
484	    # was silently deleted AFTER strict_verify passed, ALTERING the verified
485	    # claim. We now strip exactly: (1) the [#ev:id:start-end] provenance span
486	    # token, (2) the KNOWN bound entity_id LITERALS for this slot — primary_ev
487	    # plus every evidence_pool key, i.e. the exact id strings the composer
488	    # inserts as [entity_id] provenance markers — each regex-escaped, and (3) a
489	    # pure numeric [N] citation. A real qualifier word is NOT a bound entity_id,
490	    # so it never matches (2) and SURVIVES. Faithfulness-neutral: this only
491	    # prevents removal of verified substance.
492	    _known_marker_ids = {str(primary_ev)}
493	    _known_marker_ids.update(str(_k) for _k in evidence_pool.keys())
494	    _id_alt = "|".join(
495	        _re.escape(_mid)
496	        for _mid in sorted(_known_marker_ids, key=len, reverse=True)
497	        if _mid
498	    )
499	    _marker_pattern = r"\s*\[#ev:[^\]]*\]|\s*\[\d+\]"  # (1) provenance token + (3) [N] numeric
500	    if _id_alt:
501	        _marker_pattern += rf"|\s*\[(?:{_id_alt})\]"   # (2) EXACT bound entity_id literals only
502	    _marker_re = _re.compile(_marker_pattern)
503	    _clean_sentences: list[str] = []
504	    for _sv in kept:
505	        # (a) strip provenance markers ([entity_id] / [#ev:id:start-end]).
506	        _demarked = _marker_re.sub("", str(getattr(_sv, "sentence", "") or ""))
507	        # (b) drop LEADING page-furniture lines (whole-LINE only, never an
508	        # inline partial strip of a welded claim): a line is furniture iff it
509	        # is empty OR starts with a bullet/rule glyph AND carries fewer than
510	        # _KSPAN_MIN_PROSE_WORDS alpha words AND no digit. A real numeric/prose
511	        # line always clears it and stops the leading-strip.
512	        _seen_prose = False
513	        _out_lines: list[str] = []
514	        for _ln in _demarked.replace("\xad", "").split("\n"):
515	            _core = _ln.strip()
516	            if not _seen_prose:
517	                if not _core:
518	                    continue
519	                if (
520	                    _core[0] in "-*•·|–—"
521	                    and _alpha_word_count(_core) < _KSPAN_MIN_PROSE_WORDS
522	                    and not _re.search(r"\d", _core)
523	                ):
524	                    continue  # leading furniture line
525	                _seen_prose = True
526	            _out_lines.append(_core)
527	        _residue = " ".join(_l for _l in _out_lines if _l).strip()
528	        if _residue:
529	            _clean_sentences.append(_residue)
530	    if not _clean_sentences:
531	        return None
532	    return f"{' '.join(_clean_sentences)}[{marker_num}]"
533

535	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
536	# N5-FIX-2 (I-deepfix-001 wave-2): render-seam fragment-snap wellformedness gate.
537	# Defense-in-depth for slot-field VALUES cut MID source sentence (which the
538	# producer-side N5-FIX-1 alone cannot repair). When ON, a deterministic-stream
539	# kept SV that is a mid-sentence fragment of its cited source span is EXPANDED to
540	# the full covering source sentence(s) and RE-GATED through the UNCHANGED
541	# `verify_sentence_provenance`; the SV is replaced ONLY if the expanded form
542	# verifies, else the original SV is kept untouched. NEVER drops, never suppresses.
543	# DEFAULT OFF => kept list returned untouched (byte-identical).
544	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
545	_SLOT_FRAGMENT_SNAP_ON_VALUES = frozenset({"1", "true", "on", "yes"})

548	def _slot_fragment_snap_enabled() -> bool:
549	    """N5-FIX-2 — True only when ``PG_SLOT_FRAGMENT_SNAP`` is an explicit truthy
550	    value (1/true/on/yes). Unset / empty / other => OFF => byte-identical."""
551	    return (
552	        os.getenv("PG_SLOT_FRAGMENT_SNAP", "").strip().lower()
553	        in _SLOT_FRAGMENT_SNAP_ON_VALUES
554	    )

557	def _ws_tolerant_locate(needle: str, haystack: str) -> tuple[int, int] | None:
558	    """Return the (start, end) CHAR offsets of ``needle`` inside ``haystack``,
559	    comparing whitespace-tolerantly (runs of whitespace match ``\\s+``). Returns
560	    None when not found. Character-exact otherwise (no fuzzy matching)."""
561	    import re as _re  # noqa: PLC0415
562	    needle = (needle or "").strip()
563	    if not needle:
564	        return None
565	    toks = [t for t in needle.split() if t]
566	    if not toks:
567	        return None
568	    pat = r"\s+".join(_re.escape(t) for t in toks)
569	    m = _re.search(pat, haystack or "")
570	    if m is None:
571	        return None
572	    return (m.start(), m.end())
573	
574	
575	def _covering_source_sentence_span(
576	    direct_quote: str, start: int, end: int, split_fn: Any,
577	) -> tuple[int, int] | None:
578	    """Given a [start, end) fragment offset inside ``direct_quote``, return the
579	    (cstart, cend) offsets of the FULL source sentence(s) that cover it, using
580	    ``split_fn`` (``split_into_sentences``). None when no covering sentence is
581	    located."""
582	    sents = split_fn(direct_quote) or []
583	    cursor = 0
584	    cstart: int | None = None
585	    cend: int | None = None
586	    for s in sents:
587	        if not s:
588	            continue
589	        idx = direct_quote.find(s, cursor)
590	        if idx < 0:
591	            loc = _ws_tolerant_locate(s, direct_quote[cursor:])
592	            if loc is None:
593	                continue
594	            s0, s1 = cursor + loc[0], cursor + loc[1]
595	        else:
596	            s0, s1 = idx, idx + len(s)
597	        cursor = s1
598	        # Overlap test against the fragment span.
599	        if s1 > start and s0 < end:
600	            if cstart is None:
601	                cstart = s0
602	            cend = s1
603	    if cstart is None or cend is None:
604	        return None
605	    return (cstart, cend)
606	
607	
608	def _snap_one_fragment_sv(sv: Any, evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
609	                          verify_fn: Any, split_fn: Any) -> Any | None:
610	    """N5-FIX-2 core: if ``sv`` (exactly one provenance token) is a mid-sentence
611	    fragment of its cited source span, return a NEW re-verified SV expanded to the
612	    full covering source sentence(s); else None (caller keeps the original)."""
613	    import re as _re  # noqa: PLC0415
614	    toks = getattr(sv, "tokens", None) or []
615	    if len(toks) != 1:
616	        return None
617	    tok = toks[0]
618	    eid = getattr(tok, "evidence_id", "")
619	    row = evidence_pool.get(eid) or {}
620	    dq = str(row.get("direct_quote") or "")
621	    if not dq:
622	        return None
623	    raw_sentence = str(getattr(sv, "sentence", "") or "")
624	    # Strip provenance span tokens, then drop terminal punctuation for the locate.
625	    text = _re.sub(r"\s*\[#ev:[^\]]*\]", "", raw_sentence).strip()
626	    text = text.rstrip(".!?").strip()
627	    if not text:
628	        return None
629	    label_prefix = ""
630	    loc = _ws_tolerant_locate(text, dq)
631	    if loc is None:
632	        # Defensive: strip a leading "<Label>: " prefix and retry with the bare value.
633	        m = _re.match(r"^([^:]{1,60}):\s+(.*)$", text, _re.DOTALL)
634	        if m:
635	            cand = m.group(2).strip()
636	            loc2 = _ws_tolerant_locate(cand, dq)
637	            if loc2 is not None:
638	                label_prefix = m.group(1) + ": "
639	                text = cand
640	                loc = loc2
641	    if loc is None:
642	        return None
643	    start, end = loc
644	    _pre = dq[:start].rstrip()
645	    _before_frag = bool(_pre) and _pre[-1] not in ".!?"
646	    _after_frag = end < len(dq) and dq[end] not in ".!?"
647	    if not (_before_frag or _after_frag):
648	        return None  # already a complete source sentence — pass through untouched
649	    cov = _covering_source_sentence_span(dq, start, end, split_fn)
650	    if cov is None:
651	        return None
652	    cstart, cend = cov
653	    expanded = dq[cstart:cend]
654	    if not expanded.strip():
655	        return None
656	    new_tok = f"[#ev:{eid}:{cstart}-{cend}]"
657	    if expanded[-1] in ".!?":
658	        body = f"{expanded[:-1].rstrip()} {new_tok}{expanded[-1]}"
659	    else:
660	        body = f"{expanded} {new_tok}."
661	    new_sentence = f"{label_prefix}{body}"
662	    new_sv = verify_fn(new_sentence, evidence_pool)
663	    if getattr(new_sv, "is_verified", False):
664	        return new_sv
665	    return None
666

668	def _snap_fragment_kept_svs(kept_svs: list[Any],
669	                            evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]]) -> list[Any]:
670	    """N5-FIX-2 pass over a deterministic-stream kept-SV list. Flag OFF =>
671	    returns the SAME list object untouched (byte-identical). Flag ON => replaces
672	    each mid-sentence fragment SV with its verified full-source-sentence
673	    expansion; a non-expandable or non-verifying SV is kept verbatim (never
674	    dropped)."""
675	    if not _slot_fragment_snap_enabled():
676	        return kept_svs
677	    try:
678	        from .provenance_generator import (  # noqa: PLC0415
679	            verify_sentence_provenance as _verify_fn,
680	            split_into_sentences as _split_fn,
681	        )
682	    except Exception:
683	        return kept_svs
684	    out: list[Any] = []
685	    n_snapped = 0
686	    for sv in kept_svs:
687	        snapped = None
688	        try:
689	            snapped = _snap_one_fragment_sv(sv, evidence_pool, _verify_fn, _split_fn)
690	        except Exception:
691	            snapped = None
692	        if snapped is not None:
693	            out.append(snapped)
694	            n_snapped += 1
695	        else:
696	            out.append(sv)
697	    if n_snapped:
698	        logger.info(
699	            "[contract_section] N5 fragment-snap: expanded %d mid-sentence "
700	            "fragment(s) to the full covering source sentence (re-verified, "
701	            "none dropped)", n_snapped,
702	        )
703	    return out

706	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
707	# N2 (I-deepfix-001 wave-2): fragment-prose CONSOLIDATION dedup. The V30
708	# two-tier renderer emits every slot field TWICE from ONE payload — a
709	# deterministic "<Label>: <value> [ev]." fragment AND a narrative prose
710	# restatement of the same value with the same cite. When ON, a deterministic
711	# fragment whose value is fully SUBSUMED (value-substring + cite-subset +
712	# number-subset) by a NON-fragment prose sentence in the SAME slot is dropped —
713	# CONSOLIDATION, not content loss (the prose carries the identical value + every
714	# citation + every number, enforced by the three guards). A minimum-retention
715	# guard never strands an entity. Prose is NEVER dropped. DEFAULT OFF (0) =>
716	# byte-identical. Runs strictly AFTER verification on already-kept SVs.
717	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
718	_FRAGMENT_PROSE_DEDUP_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"0", "false", "off", "no", ""})
719	
720	
721	def _fragment_prose_dedup_enabled() -> bool:
722	    """N2 — True unless ``PG_CONTRACT_FRAGMENT_PROSE_DEDUP`` is set to a falsey
723	    value. DEFAULT "0" (OFF => byte-identical); same falsey vocabulary as
724	    ``_false_gap_kspan_enabled`` (Codex iarch007 P1 #3)."""
725	    return (
726	        os.getenv("PG_CONTRACT_FRAGMENT_PROSE_DEDUP", "0").strip().lower()
727	        not in _FRAGMENT_PROSE_DEDUP_OFF_VALUES
728	    )
729	
730	
731	def _dedup_fragment_prose_restatements(
732	    kept_sentences: list[Any],
733	    det_sv_ids: set[int],
734	    known_labels: set[str],
735	    entity_to_slot_id: dict[str, str],
736	) -> tuple[list[Any], dict[str, int]]:
737	    """N2 core (pure, no LLM / no re-verify). Drop deterministic-stream fragment
738	    SVs whose value is subsumed by a same-slot non-fragment prose sentence. Returns
739	    (new_kept, telemetry). Prose is never dropped; a distinct / uncontained /
740	    cite-or-number-superset fragment is kept; the minimum-retention guard keeps a
741	    fragment that is its entity's sole substantive kept sentence."""
742	    from .fact_dedup import (  # noqa: PLC0415
743	        _CITATION_TOKEN_RE,
744	        _nli_cite_set,
745	        _nli_num_set,
746	    )
747	
748	    def _slot_of(sv: Any) -> Any:
749	        toks = getattr(sv, "tokens", None) or []
750	        if not toks:
751	            return None
752	        _ov = getattr(sv, "reanchor_original_slot_id", None)
753	        return _ov if _ov is not None else entity_to_slot_id.get(toks[0].evidence_id)
754	
755	    def _primary_eid(sv: Any) -> Any:
756	        toks = getattr(sv, "tokens", None) or []
757	        return toks[0].evidence_id if toks else None
758	
759	    def _normalize(s: Any) -> str:
760	        s = _CITATION_TOKEN_RE.sub(" ", str(s or ""))
761	        return " ".join(s.split()).casefold()
762	
763	    labels_cf = sorted(
764	        {str(lbl).casefold() for lbl in known_labels if str(lbl).strip()},
765	        key=len, reverse=True,
766	    )
767	    # Classify fragments: id(sv) -> (value_norm, cite_set, num_set).
768	    frag_info: dict[int, tuple[str, frozenset, frozenset]] = {}
769	    for sv in kept_sentences:
770	        if id(sv) not in det_sv_ids:
771	            continue
772	        cleaned = _normalize(getattr(sv, "sentence", ""))
773	        for lab in labels_cf:
774	            prefix = lab + ":"
775	            if cleaned.startswith(prefix):
776	                # Strip the fragment's OWN trailing sentence punctuation (the
777	                # renderer emits "<Label>: <value> [ev]." so the period is the
778	                # sentence terminator, never part of the field value). Without
779	                # this, a value that appears MID-sentence in the covering prose
780	                # (e.g. "US labor markets," in P1) would fail the substring test.
781	                value_norm = cleaned[len(prefix):].strip().rstrip(" .!?;:,").strip()
782	                sent = getattr(sv, "sentence", "")
783	                frag_info[id(sv)] = (
784	                    value_norm, _nli_cite_set(sent), _nli_num_set(sent),
785	                )
786	                break
787	    if not frag_info:
788	        return kept_sentences, {"n_fragments_dropped": 0}
789	
790	    # Substantive-kept count per PRIMARY (tokens[0]) evidence_id — mirrors the
791	    # `_kept_substantive_by_entity` attribution rule (non-gap sentences only).
792	    subst: dict[Any, int] = {}
793	    for sv in kept_sentences:
794	        if _is_gap_disclosure_sentence(getattr(sv, "sentence", "")):
795	            continue
796	        eid = _primary_eid(sv)
797	        if eid is not None:
798	            subst[eid] = subst.get(eid, 0) + 1
799	
800	    drop_ids: set[int] = set()
801	    n_dropped = 0
802	    for sv in kept_sentences:
803	        fi = frag_info.get(id(sv))
804	        if fi is None:
805	            continue
806	        value_norm, frag_cites, frag_nums = fi
807	        if not value_norm:
808	            continue  # empty value never matches (guard against substring-of-all)
809	        frag_slot = _slot_of(sv)
810	        frag_eid = _primary_eid(sv)
811	        matched = False
812	        for cand in kept_sentences:
813	            if cand is sv or id(cand) in frag_info or id(cand) in drop_ids:
814	                continue  # candidate must be a non-fragment, not-yet-dropped SV
815	            if _slot_of(cand) != frag_slot:
816	                continue
817	            cand_sent = getattr(cand, "sentence", "")
818	            if (
819	                value_norm in _normalize(cand_sent)
820	                and frag_cites <= _nli_cite_set(cand_sent)
821	                and frag_nums <= _nli_num_set(cand_sent)
822	            ):
823	                matched = True
824	                break
825	        if not matched:
826	            continue
827	        # Minimum-retention guard: never strand the fragment's own entity.
828	        if frag_eid is not None and subst.get(frag_eid, 0) - 1 < 1:
829	            continue
830	        drop_ids.add(id(sv))
831	        n_dropped += 1
832	        if frag_eid is not None:
833	            subst[frag_eid] = subst.get(frag_eid, 0) - 1
834	    if not drop_ids:
835	        return kept_sentences, {"n_fragments_dropped": 0}
836	    new_kept = [sv for sv in kept_sentences if id(sv) not in drop_ids]
837	    return new_kept, {"n_fragments_dropped": n_dropped}

841	# N4 (I-deepfix-001 wave-2): the zero-kept contract slot gap-DISCLOSURE sentence.
842	# The disclosure is CORRECT (disclose-not-drop, §-1.3); the bug is its audience
843	# register — the legacy template interpolates the raw entity SLUG + two internal
844	# filenames + "curator-actionable" jargon into reader prose. Flag ON swaps the
845	# reader-facing STRING for plain English (no slug/filename/jargon/underscore),
846	# keeping the [N] citation; every machine detail stays where auditors read it
847	# (manifest.frame_coverage_report, human_gap_tasks.json, slot_drop_log). DEFAULT
848	# OFF (0) => byte-identical legacy template.
849	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
850	_CONTRACT_GAP_PLAIN_OFF_VALUES = frozenset({"0", "false", "off", "no", ""})
851	
852	
853	def _contract_gap_plain_disclosure_enabled() -> bool:
854	    """N4 — True unless ``PG_CONTRACT_GAP_PLAIN_DISCLOSURE`` is set falsey.
855	    DEFAULT "0" (OFF => byte-identical); same falsey vocabulary as the sibling
856	    ``_false_gap_kspan_enabled``."""
857	    return (
858	        os.getenv("PG_CONTRACT_GAP_PLAIN_DISCLOSURE", "0").strip().lower()
859	        not in _CONTRACT_GAP_PLAIN_OFF_VALUES
860	    )
861	
862	
863	def _contract_gap_sentence(primary_ev: str, marker: str, label: str = "") -> str:
864	    """N4 — build the zero-kept-slot gap disclosure sentence.
865	
866	    Flag OFF (default): return the EXACT legacy byte string (both the primary_ev
867	    and the no-primary_ev branch) — byte-identical.
868	
869	    Flag ON: return a plain-English disclosure with NO slug, NO internal
870	    filename, NO "curator-actionable" jargon, NO underscore, keeping the {marker}
871	    [N] citation. The disclosure REMAINS in both modes (disclose-not-drop,
872	    §-1.3); only the reader register changes. ``label`` (the ev.statement →
873	    ev.title → contract_entity.label_name chain) is accepted for an optional
874	    "(source: <label>)" suffix but intentionally left unused — the slot heading
875	    already carries the human subsection title, so the sentence is valid without
876	    it."""
877	    if _contract_gap_plain_disclosure_enabled():
878	        base = (
879	            "Insufficient verified evidence for this item: no content drawn "
880	            "from the bound source passed verification, so no findings are "
881	            "reported here."
882	        )
883	        return f"{base}{marker}" if primary_ev else base
884	    if primary_ev:
885	        return (
886	            f"Contract-bound content for {primary_ev} did not "
887	            f"survive strict verification against retrieved "
888	            f"primary source text; this slot is a curator-"
889	            f"actionable gap. See manifest.frame_coverage_report "
890	            f"and human_gap_tasks.json for per-entity detail."
891	            f"{marker}"
892	        )
893	    return (
894	        "Contract-bound content did not survive strict "
895	        "verification; curator-actionable gap."
896	    )

900	# B5 (I-deepfix-001 wave-2): hollow contract-slot re-anchor to a same-work CLEAN
901	# sibling, and author/abstract chrome-header strip. When a slot renders as a gap
902	# because its bound entity's ONLY copy is chrome-contaminated ("## Author Listed …
903	# ## Abstract We study…") or unbound, bind by DOI (+ title/author) to a clean
904	# same-work sibling row and render ITS verbatim span through the UNCHANGED
905	# strict_verify K-span (`_kspan_fallback_body`). Faithfulness-STRENGTHENING: a
906	# verified verbatim span of the SAME work replaces a false gap; the verifier is
907	# byte-untouched. All three flags DEFAULT OFF => the pass never runs =>
908	# byte-identical, and N4 backstops any residual honest gap.
909	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
910	_B5_ON_VALUES = frozenset({"1", "true", "on", "yes"})
911	_B5_DOI_RE = re.compile(r"10\.\d{4,9}/[^\s\"'<>]+")
912	# The "## Author Listed … ## Abstract" chrome prefix (both headers required, from
913	# string start) — conservative so a legitimate quote merely mentioning "abstract"
914	# is never stripped.
915	_B5_AUTHOR_ABSTRACT_PREFIX_RE = re.compile(
916	    r"^\s*#{1,6}\s*author\s+listed\b.*?#{1,6}\s*abstract\b[:\s]*",
917	    re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL,
918	)
919	
920	
921	def _author_abstract_header_strip_enabled() -> bool:
922	    """B5 — True only when ``PG_AUTHOR_ABSTRACT_HEADER_STRIP`` is truthy. DEFAULT OFF."""
923	    return os.getenv("PG_AUTHOR_ABSTRACT_HEADER_STRIP", "").strip().lower() in _B5_ON_VALUES
924	
925	
926	def _contract_bind_doi_fallback_enabled() -> bool:
927	    """B5 — True only when ``PG_CONTRACT_BIND_DOI_FALLBACK`` is truthy. DEFAULT OFF."""
928	    return os.getenv("PG_CONTRACT_BIND_DOI_FALLBACK", "").strip().lower() in _B5_ON_VALUES
929	
930	
931	def _contract_reanchor_clean_sibling_enabled() -> bool:
932	    """B5 — True only when ``PG_CONTRACT_REANCHOR_CLEAN_SIBLING`` is truthy. DEFAULT OFF."""
933	    return os.getenv("PG_CONTRACT_REANCHOR_CLEAN_SIBLING", "").strip().lower() in _B5_ON_VALUES
934	
935	
936	def _b5_reanchor_any_enabled() -> bool:
937	    """B5 — True iff ANY of the three B5 flags is on (the gate the gap-emit loop
938	    consults; all OFF => the whole pass is skipped => byte-identical)."""
939	    return (
940	        _author_abstract_header_strip_enabled()
941	        or _contract_bind_doi_fallback_enabled()
942	        or _contract_reanchor_clean_sibling_enabled()
943	    )
944	
945	
946	def _strip_author_abstract_header(quote: str) -> str:
947	    """B5 — strip a leading "## Author Listed … ## Abstract" markdown chrome prefix,
948	    returning the abstract body onward. Flag OFF or no such prefix => the quote is
949	    returned UNCHANGED (byte-identical)."""
950	    if not _author_abstract_header_strip_enabled():
951	        return quote
952	    m = _B5_AUTHOR_ABSTRACT_PREFIX_RE.match(quote or "")
953	    if m is None:
954	        return quote
955	    return (quote or "")[m.end():].lstrip()
956	
957	
958	def _b5_norm_doi(row: dict[str, Any] | None) -> str:
959	    """B5 — normalized lowercase DOI for a pool row: the ``doi`` field (doi.org
960	    prefix stripped) else a DOI parsed from url/source_url. "" when none."""
961	    doi = str((row or {}).get("doi") or "").strip()
962	    if doi:
963	        doi = re.sub(r"(?i)^https?://(dx\.)?doi\.org/", "", doi).strip()
964	        if doi:
965	            return doi.lower()
966	    for _k in ("url", "source_url"):
967	        m = _B5_DOI_RE.search(str((row or {}).get(_k) or ""))
968	        if m:
969	            return m.group(0).lower()
970	    return ""
971	
972	
973	def _b5_norm_title(row: dict[str, Any] | None) -> str:
974	    """B5 — whitespace-collapsed casefolded title for a pool row ("" when none)."""
975	    return " ".join(str((row or {}).get("title") or "").split()).casefold()
976	
977	
978	def _b5_entity_doi(contract_entity: Any) -> str:
979	    """B5 — DOI from the RequiredEntity (``doi`` / ``identifier``), normalized."""
980	    if contract_entity is None:
981	        return ""
982	    for _attr in ("doi", "identifier"):
983	        v = getattr(contract_entity, _attr, None)
984	        if v:
985	            m = _B5_DOI_RE.search(str(v))
986	            if m:
987	                return m.group(0).lower()
988	            return _b5_norm_doi({"doi": str(v)})
989	    return ""
990	
991	
992	def _b5_entity_title(contract_entity: Any) -> str:
993	    """B5 — title from the RequiredEntity (``title`` / ``label_name``), normalized."""
994	    if contract_entity is None:
995	        return ""
996	    for _attr in ("title", "label_name"):
997	        v = getattr(contract_entity, _attr, None)
998	        if v:
999	            return " ".join(str(v).split()).casefold()
1000	    return ""
1001	
1002	
1003	def _b5_row_cleanliness(row: dict[str, Any] | None) -> float:
1004	    """B5 — a 'clean copy' score for sibling ranking (crw / authority / credibility;
1005	    0.0 when none carry one)."""
1006	    for _k in ("content_relevance_weight", "crw", "authority_score", "credibility_weight"):
1007	        v = (row or {}).get(_k)
1008	        if isinstance(v, (int, float)):
1009	            return float(v)
1010	    return 0.0

1013	def _b5_same_work_siblings(
1014	    primary_ev: str,
1015	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
1016	    target_doi: str,
1017	    target_title: str,
1018	) -> list[str]:
1019	    """B5 — evidence_ids of OTHER pool rows that are the SAME work as ``primary_ev``
1020	    (same normalized DOI, else same normalized non-empty title), ranked cleanest
1021	    first (cleanliness score desc, then header-stripped usable-span length desc)."""
1022	    out: list[str] = []
1023	    for eid, row in (evidence_pool or {}).items():
1024	        if eid == primary_ev:
1025	            continue
1026	        rd = _b5_norm_doi(row)
1027	        rt = _b5_norm_title(row)
1028	        same = (bool(target_doi) and rd == target_doi) or (
1029	            bool(target_title) and rt == target_title
1030	        )
1031	        if same:
1032	            out.append(eid)
1033	
1034	    def _key(eid: str) -> tuple[float, int]:
1035	        row = evidence_pool.get(eid) or {}
1036	        stripped = _strip_author_abstract_header(str(row.get("direct_quote") or ""))
1037	        return (_b5_row_cleanliness(row), len(stripped.strip()))
1038	
1039	    out.sort(key=_key, reverse=True)
1040	    return out
1041

1043	def _b5_reanchor_clean_kspan(
1044	    *,
1045	    primary_ev: str,
1046	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
1047	    ev_to_num: dict[str, int],
1048	    biblio_slice: list[dict[str, Any]],
1049	    rewrite_fn: Any,
1050	    strict_verify_fn: Any,
1051	    contract_entity: Any,
1052	) -> tuple[str, str] | None:
1053	    """B5 — for a hollow slot, render a verified K-span from (1) the bound entity's
1054	    own span with the author/abstract chrome header stripped, then (2) a same-work
1055	    CLEAN sibling (bound by DOI + title/author). Each candidate span is routed
1056	    through the UNCHANGED `_kspan_fallback_body` (→ strict_verify). Returns
1057	    (body_md, chosen_ev_id) on the FIRST candidate that verifies; else None. A
1058	    bibliography entry for a newly-cited sibling is synthesized ONLY on success (no
1059	    dangling number). Never mutates the real span (works on a patched pool copy)."""
1060	    primary_row = evidence_pool.get(primary_ev) or {}
1061	    target_doi = _b5_norm_doi(primary_row) or _b5_entity_doi(contract_entity)
1062	    target_title = _b5_norm_title(primary_row) or _b5_entity_title(contract_entity)
1063	
1064	    candidates: list[str] = []
1065	    # (1) the bound entity itself — only useful with the header strip on.
1066	    if _author_abstract_header_strip_enabled():
1067	        candidates.append(primary_ev)
1068	    # (2) same-work clean siblings — the DOI/title fallback + re-anchor.
1069	    if _contract_bind_doi_fallback_enabled() or _contract_reanchor_clean_sibling_enabled():
1070	        for _sib in _b5_same_work_siblings(
1071	            primary_ev, evidence_pool, target_doi, target_title,
1072	        ):
1073	            if _sib not in candidates:
1074	                candidates.append(_sib)
1075	    if not candidates:
1076	        return None
1077	
1078	    for cand in candidates:
1079	        row = evidence_pool.get(cand) or {}
1080	        stripped = _strip_author_abstract_header(str(row.get("direct_quote") or ""))
1081	        if len(stripped.strip()) < _MIN_VERIFIABLE_SPAN_CHARS:
1082	            continue
1083	        _already = cand in ev_to_num
1084	        marker_num = ev_to_num[cand] if _already else (len(biblio_slice) + 1)
1085	        patched = dict(evidence_pool)
1086	        patched[cand] = {**row, "direct_quote": stripped}
1087	        try:
1088	            body = _kspan_fallback_body(
1089	                primary_ev=cand,
1090	                evidence_pool=patched,
1091	                marker_num=marker_num,
1092	                rewrite_fn=rewrite_fn,
1093	                strict_verify_fn=strict_verify_fn,
1094	            )
1095	        except Exception:
1096	            body = None
1097	        if not body:
1098	            continue
1099	        if not _already:
1100	            _crow = evidence_pool.get(cand, {})
1101	            _stmt = next(
1102	                (s for s in (_crow.get("statement"), _crow.get("title"), cand) if s),
1103	                cand,
1104	            )
1105	            biblio_slice.append({
1106	                "num": marker_num,
1107	                "evidence_id": cand,
1108	                "url": _crow.get("url") or _crow.get("source_url") or "",
1109	                "tier": _crow.get("tier", ""),
1110	                "statement": str(_stmt)[:300],
1111	            })
1112	            ev_to_num[cand] = marker_num
1113	        return (body, cand)
1114	    return None
1115

1117	def _basket_fallback_corroborators_for_slot(
1118	    *,
1119	    slot_entity_ids: list[str],
1120	    cluster_id_by_evidence: dict[str, list[str]] | None,
1121	    basket_supports_by_cluster: dict[str, list[str]],
1122	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
1123	    already_bound: set[str],
1124	) -> list[str]:
1125	    """A1 — same-claim corroborator evidence_ids to re-bind a slot whose
1126	    primary URL(s) came back as fetch-layer SHELLS (A1a routed them to
1127	    METADATA_ONLY/not_extractable, leaving the slot's real case-law / source
1128	    content unbound at ``v30_entity_id: None``).
1129	
1130	    Joins via the slot's own bound entity_ids -> ``cluster_id_by_evidence`` ->
1131	    the basket's independently SPAN-VERIFIED (SUPPORTS) members, the IDENTICAL
1132	    faithfulness logic as ``verified_corroborators_for_tokens``:
1133	      - SUPPORTS-only (the verified-origin members, never the advisory
1134	        clustered total);
1135	      - anti-cross-claim — only a token mapping to EXACTLY ONE cluster is
1136	        expanded (a multi-cluster source can't be attributed to ONE claim);
1137	      - a corroborator is kept ONLY if it (a) is not already bound to the slot
1138	        and (b) carries a usable verifiable span in ``evidence_pool``.
1139	
1140	    SCOPE NOTE (Codex iarch007 P1 #2): this join is keyed on the slot's bound
1141	    entity_ids. A genuinely UNRECOVERABLE slot — every bound entity is a
1142	    content-less shell that NEVER produced an extracted claim — is, by
1143	    construction, absent from ``cluster_id_by_evidence`` (the claim graph only
1144	    contains entities whose row content yielded an AtomicClaim). For such a
1145	    slot this returns [] and the slot correctly stays a disclosed gap stub.
1146	    Reaching a cluster for a fully-content-less slot would require a
1147	    MANY-cluster linkage (query_origin / section / "same required entity"),
1148	    which is cross-claim and would RELAX the basket's anti-cross-claim
1149	    faithfulness rule — out of bounds. The recoverable case (a slot whose
1150	    frame_row is a shell yet whose pool row clustered via abstract content) DOES
1151	    fire here.
1152	
1153	    FAITHFULNESS: this returns CANDIDATES to route through the UNCHANGED
1154	    slot-fill -> strict_verify path. It binds nothing itself, drops nothing,
1155	    relaxes no threshold. The basket carries tier/authority as a WEIGHT only.
1156	    Returns [] when basket data is absent => byte-identical OFF path."""
1157	    if not basket_supports_by_cluster:
1158	        return []
1159	    out: list[str] = []
1160	    seen: set[str] = set()
1161	    for _eid in slot_entity_ids:
1162	        _ccids = (cluster_id_by_evidence or {}).get(_eid, []) or []
1163	        # Anti-cross-claim: only an UNAMBIGUOUS single-cluster source may
1164	        # corroborate (mirrors verified_corroborators_for_tokens).
1165	        if len(_ccids) != 1:
1166	            continue
1167	        for _support_eid in basket_supports_by_cluster.get(_ccids[0], []):
1168	            if _support_eid in seen or _support_eid in already_bound:
1169	                continue
1170	            _pool_row = evidence_pool.get(_support_eid)
1171	            if not _pool_row:
1172	                continue
1173	            # Must carry a real verifiable span — a corroborator that is itself
1174	            # a shell / metadata-only row cannot rescue the slot.
1175	            if len(str(_pool_row.get("direct_quote") or "").strip()) < _MIN_VERIFIABLE_SPAN_CHARS:
1176	                continue
1177	            seen.add(_support_eid)
1178	            out.append(_support_eid)
1179	    return out
1180

1218	        retrieval_timings=(),
1219	    )

1222	def contract_sentence_citation_nums(
1223	    sv: Any,
1224	    tokens: list,
1225	    ev_to_num: dict[str, int],
1226	    *,
1227	    basket_supports_by_cluster: dict[str, list[str]],
1228	    cluster_id_by_evidence: Any,
1229	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
1230	    basket_by_cluster: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
1231	) -> list[int]:
1232	    """The V30 contract slot-regroup's per-sentence citation-number decision, extracted so the
1233	    benchmark render path's attachment logic is behaviorally testable WITHOUT the async
1234	    LLM-driven ``run_contract_section`` machinery (§-1.4: prove the effect fires in the OUTPUT,
1235	    not that a string is present).
1236	
1237	    Returns the ordered list of bibliography numbers attached to THIS sentence:
1238	      * its OWN strict-verified tokens (minus relevance-demoted/refuted eids), then
1239	      * each basket corroborator whose CLAIM-LOCAL span grounds THIS sentence
1240	        (``corroborator_grounds_sentence_via_basket`` — the SAME single decision the legacy
1241	        resolver applies, so a corroborator filtered off S1 can NOT be reattached to S1 via the
1242	        section-wide ``ev_to_num`` it earned on S2). Faithfulness-TIGHTENING; engine untouched.
1243	    OFF path (empty ``basket_supports_by_cluster``) => only own tokens => byte-identical regroup.
1244	    """
1245	    from .provenance_generator import (  # noqa: PLC0415
1246	        verified_corroborators_with_clusters_for_tokens,
1247	        corroborator_grounds_sentence_via_basket,
1248	        _verifier_cleaned_text,
1249	        _citation_nli_purity_enabled,
1250	        _own_token_span_reasserts,
1251	    )
1252	
1253	    _demoted_eids = (
1254	        (getattr(sv, "relevance_demoted_eids", None) or frozenset())
1255	        | (getattr(sv, "relevance_refuted_eids", None) or frozenset())
1256	    )
1257	    _corro_claim_text = _verifier_cleaned_text(getattr(sv, "sentence", "") or "")
1258	    used_nums: list[int] = []
1259	    # I-deepfix-001 P2 (#1344): OWN-TOKEN bounds+overlap re-assert on the V30 contract regroup.
1260	    # Gate-B forces THIS path (not the flat resolver body), so the render-time own-token purity
1261	    # re-assert MUST also run here — a withheld bad own token is still registered in
1262	    # ev_to_num / biblio_slice by the resolver (line ~1615), so without this it would be
1263	    # reattached into the SHIPPED slot body and the render-time purity fix would be a no-op on
1264	    # the benchmark path. Mirror the resolver's own-token pass/withhold + minimum-retention guard
1265	    # EXACTLY (``resolve_provenance_to_citations_with_count``): re-assert each own token's stored
1266	    # (start,end) against the CURRENT ``direct_quote`` via the SAME predicate, WITHHOLD its inline
1267	    # [N] on failure (the source STAYS in the bibliography), and never let a sentence go
1268	    # cited->uncited. The withhold DECISION here is deterministic and identical to the resolver's
1269	    # on the same tokens, and the resolver already recorded the purity telemetry on that decision
1270	    # (its discarded flat body) — so we ACT here WITHOUT a second telemetry increment, which also
1271	    # keeps the withheld_own_token count from being inflated by the two paths. OFF (=0) =>
1272	    # ``_own_token_span_reasserts`` skipped => byte-identical legacy own-token regroup.
1273	    _purity_on = _citation_nli_purity_enabled()
1274	    _own_pass: list[int] = []
1275	    _own_withheld: list[int] = []
1276	    for tok in tokens:
1277	        if tok.evidence_id in _demoted_eids:
1278	            continue
1279	        n = ev_to_num.get(tok.evidence_id)
1280	        if n is None:
1281	            continue
1282	        if _purity_on and not _own_token_span_reasserts(
1283	            tok, evidence_pool, _corro_claim_text
1284	        ):
1285	            if n not in _own_withheld:
1286	                _own_withheld.append(n)
1287	        elif n not in _own_pass:
1288	            _own_pass.append(n)
1289	    # MINIMUM-RETENTION GUARD (same as the resolver): never strand a sentence's own support —
1290	    # if EVERY surviving own token would be withheld, KEEP them all (never cited->uncited).
1291	    if _purity_on and _own_withheld and not _own_pass:
1292	        _own_pass = list(_own_withheld)
1293	        _own_withheld = []
1294	    for n in _own_pass:
1295	        if n not in used_nums:
1296	            used_nums.append(n)
1297	    for _corro_eid, _corro_ccid in verified_corroborators_with_clusters_for_tokens(
1298	        [tok.evidence_id for tok in tokens],
1299	        basket_supports_by_cluster=basket_supports_by_cluster,
1300	        cluster_id_by_evidence=cluster_id_by_evidence,
1301	        evidence_pool=evidence_pool,
1302	    ):
1303	        if _corro_eid in _demoted_eids:
1304	            continue
1305	        if not corroborator_grounds_sentence_via_basket(
1306	            _corro_claim_text, _corro_eid, basket_by_cluster,
1307	            selected_cluster_id=_corro_ccid,
1308	        ):
1309	            continue
1310	        n = ev_to_num.get(_corro_eid)
1311	        if n is not None and n not in used_nums:
1312	            used_nums.append(n)
1313	    return used_nums

1316	def _contract_dedup_enabled() -> bool:
1317	    """I-wire-014 (#1336): outer gate for the CONTRACT-section consolidate-keep-all
1318	    dedup. Mirrors the SAME flags the multi_section path's anti-restatement pass
1319	    routes through inside ``fact_dedup.build_groups`` — the literal/Jaccard prose
1320	    seam (``PG_FACT_DEDUP_PROSE``) and the NLI mutual-entailment prose seam
1321	    (``PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_PROSE``). DEFAULT-OFF on BOTH => the contract path is
1322	    byte-identical to today. ON => the restatement cluster (e.g. the drb_72 "probability
1323	    of computerisation ... Gaussian process classifier" x8 in Empirical_Displacement) is
1324	    consolidated by KEEP-FIRST-VERBATIM index drops — the primary restatement is kept
1325	    verbatim and redundant occurrences whose citation set is a SUBSET of the survivor's
1326	    are dropped, so every citation of the merged members survives on the kept primary
1327	    (§-1.3 CONSOLIDATE-keep-all); no LLM rewrite/cross-reference is produced. Read here so
1328	    the outer block self-skips with zero side effects when the operator hasn't turned the
1329	    seam on; ``build_groups`` re-reads the same flags internally to decide which prose
1330	    clusterer fires."""
1331	
1332	    def _on(name: str) -> bool:
1333	        return os.getenv(name, "0").strip().lower() not in ("", "0", "false", "off", "no")
1334	
1335	    return _on("PG_FACT_DEDUP_PROSE") or _on("PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI_PROSE")

1338	async def _consolidate_contract_section_sentences(
1339	    section_title: str,
1340	    kept_sentences: list[Any],
1341	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
1342	    *,
1343	    strict_verify_fn: Any,
1344	    dedup_llm_callable: Any,
1345	) -> tuple[list[Any], dict[str, Any]]:
1346	    """Consolidate degenerate intra-section restatements on ONE contract section by
1347	    KEEP-FIRST-VERBATIM index drops — no LLM rewrite, no re-verify, faithfulness FROZEN.
1348	
1349	    I-wire-014 (#1336 / #4b): the contract path's keystone consolidation. ``kept_sentences``
1350	    are SentenceVerification objects whose ``.sentence`` carries the PRE-resolve
1351	    ``[#ev:id:start-end]`` provenance tokens; they are POSITIONALLY aligned with the section
1352	    sentence list fed to ``fact_dedup.build_groups`` (index i <-> kept_sentences[i]). We run
1353	    the SAME gated prose clusterers the certified multi_section path uses (literal/Jaccard +
1354	    bidirectional-NLI, behind PG_FACT_DEDUP_PROSE / PG_CONSOLIDATION_NLI[_PROSE]) and keep the
1355	    PRIMARY restatement of each cluster VERBATIM (already upstream-verified, so it can never
1356	    fail a re-verify), dropping a redundant by its OCCURRENCE INDEX only when its citation SET
1357	    and numbers are a SUBSET of the primary's. CONSOLIDATE-KEEP-ALL (§-1.3): the kept primary
1358	    already carries every citation + number of every dropped redundant, byte-identical
1359	    duplicates collapse (distinct indexes), and nothing distinct, cited, or numeric is lost.
1360	
1361	    ``strict_verify_fn`` and ``dedup_llm_callable`` are accepted for call-site stability but
1362	    are UNUSED on this keep-first-verbatim path (no rewrite is produced, so nothing needs
1363	    re-verification and no LLM is called). The outer ``_contract_dedup_enabled()`` gate
1364	    (default-OFF) governs whether this runs at all.
1365	
1366	    Returns (new_kept_sentences, telemetry).
1367	    """
1368	
1369	    # Single-section feed. fact_dedup keys by section title; the prose paths
1370	    # cluster WITHIN a section (the NLI path requires m.section == primary.section),
1371	    # so a one-section dict is exactly what consolidates the intra-section restatements.
1372	    # The list is POSITIONALLY aligned with ``kept_sentences`` (index i <-> kept_sentences[i]),
1373	    # which is what makes the index-based drop below correct even for byte-identical duplicates.
1374	    sentence_strs: list[str] = [sv.sentence for sv in kept_sentences]
1375	    sections_for_dedup: dict[str, list[str]] = {section_title: sentence_strs}
1376	
1377	    # I-wire-014 #4b (Codex diff-gate iter-2 P1 fix): keep-first-VERBATIM, NO LLM rewrite, drops keyed by
1378	    # OCCURRENCE INDEX — never by sentence STRING. The prior approach (fact_dedup.dedup_pass) ran an LLM
1379	    # cross-ref REWRITE that drifts on the terse contract slot sentences and FAILS strict_verify, so the
1380	    # revert-on-fail fallback reverted EVERY cluster and the degenerate restatements never collapsed
1381	    # ("probability of computerisation" x17 survived). A string-keyed drop ALSO cannot collapse a
1382	    # byte-identical duplicate (dropping the shared string would delete the primary too) — the exact case
1383	    # the operator flagged. ROBUST FIX: use the redundancy GROUPS directly (build_groups = the gated
1384	    # Jaccard + bidirectional-NLI prose clusterers, the SAME clusterers the certified multi_section path
1385	    # uses) and KEEP the PRIMARY restatement VERBATIM (already upstream strict_verify-passed, so no
1386	    # re-verify can ever drop it). DROP a redundant by its list INDEX only when (a) it is in this section,
1387	    # (b) it is NOT the primary's index and is no group's primary slot, and (c) its citation SET is a
1388	    # SUBSET of the primary's AND its numbers are a SUBSET. Because the kept primary already carries every
1389	    # citation + number the dropped redundant had, §-1.3 CONSOLIDATE-KEEP-ALL holds: nothing distinct,
1390	    # cited, or numeric is ever lost, byte-identical duplicates DO collapse (distinct indexes), and there
1391	    # is no rewrite that can fail the faithfulness gate (strict_verify / NLI / 4-role / span UNCHANGED).
1392	    from .fact_dedup import (  # noqa: PLC0415
1393	        build_groups as _build_groups,
1394	        _nli_cite_set as _cite_set,
1395	        _nli_num_set as _num_set,
1396	        _CITATION_TOKEN_RE as _cite_strip_re,
1397	    )
1398	
1399	    n_kept = len(kept_sentences)

content.content:
1400	    groups = _build_groups(sections_for_dedup, section_order=[section_title])
1401	    # Every slot that is SOME group's primary is protected from being dropped, even if a different
1402	    # (cross-path) group lists the same index as a redundant — the verbatim primary must always survive.
1403	    primary_idxs: set[int] = {
1404	        g.primary.index
1405	        for g in groups
1406	        if g.primary.section == section_title and 0 <= g.primary.index < n_kept
1407	    }
1408	    drop_idx: set[int] = set()
1409	    n_groups_used = 0
1410	    for g in groups:
1411	        primary = g.primary
1412	        if primary.section != section_title or not (0 <= primary.index < n_kept):
1413	            continue
1414	        p_cites = _cite_set(primary.sentence)
1415	        p_nums = _num_set(primary.sentence)
1416	        grp_dropped = 0
1417	        for r in g.redundants:
1418	            if (
1419	                r.section == section_title
1420	                and 0 <= r.index < n_kept
1421	                and r.index != primary.index      # never drop the primary's own slot
1422	                and r.index not in primary_idxs   # never drop ANY group's primary slot
1423	                and r.index not in drop_idx       # idempotent
1424	                and _cite_set(r.sentence) <= p_cites   # no citation lost (primary carries them)
1425	                and _num_set(r.sentence) <= p_nums      # no distinct number lost
1426	            ):
1427	                drop_idx.add(r.index)
1428	                grp_dropped += 1
1429	        if grp_dropped:
1430	            n_groups_used += 1
1431	
1432	    # I-wire-014 #4b (Codex diff-gate iter-3 P1): build_groups' numeric path emits ONLY CROSS-section
1433	    # groups (fact_dedup.py:891 distinct_sections<2 skip) and the prose/Jaccard + NLI paths skip
1434	    # non-empty numeric signatures, so a sentence carrying a %/$/year restated VERBATIM within ONE
1435	    # contract section forms no group and never collapses (common in clinical/regulatory contract prose).
1436	    # A byte-identical restatement (modulo citation tokens + whitespace/case) is UNAMBIGUOUSLY the same
1437	    # claim — collapse it here under the SAME §-1.3 guard. Survivor = the occurrence carrying the SUPERSET
1438	    # citation set (ties -> earliest); a duplicate occurrence is dropped only when its cite-set is a SUBSET
1439	    # of the survivor's (numbers identical by construction). Identical text => zero false-merge risk, so
1440	    # the architecture's deliberate intra-section numeric DISTINCT-claim protection (which guards DIFFERENT
1441	    # text sharing a number) is untouched. A slot that is any build_groups primary is never dropped here.
1442	    def _exact_norm(_s: str) -> str:
1443	        return " ".join(_cite_strip_re.sub(" ", _s).split()).lower()
1444	
1445	    by_norm: dict[str, list[int]] = {}
1446	    for i, sv in enumerate(kept_sentences):
1447	        by_norm.setdefault(_exact_norm(sv.sentence), []).append(i)
1448	    for norm_text, idxs in by_norm.items():
1449	        if not norm_text or len(idxs) < 2:
1450	            continue
1451	        cites_by_idx = {i: _cite_set(kept_sentences[i].sentence) for i in idxs}
1452	        # survivor = largest cite-set, ties broken to the earliest occurrence; never dropped.
1453	        survivor = max(idxs, key=lambda i: (len(cites_by_idx[i]), -i))
1454	        s_cites = cites_by_idx[survivor]
1455	        grp_dropped = 0
1456	        for i in idxs:
1457	            if (
1458	                i != survivor
1459	                and i not in drop_idx
1460	                and i not in primary_idxs       # never drop a verbatim build_groups primary
1461	                and cites_by_idx[i] <= s_cites  # no citation lost (survivor carries them)
1462	            ):
1463	                drop_idx.add(i)
1464	                grp_dropped += 1
1465	        if grp_dropped:
1466	            n_groups_used += 1
1467	
1468	    telemetry = {
1469	        "n_groups": n_groups_used,
1470	        "n_redundants": len(drop_idx),
1471	        "n_rewrites_applied": 0,           # keep-first-verbatim => NO LLM rewrite
1472	        "n_rewrites_verified_drop": 0,     # primary kept verbatim => no re-verify can drop it
1473	        "contract_dedup_mode": "keep_first_verbatim",
1474	    }
1475	    if not drop_idx:
1476	        return list(kept_sentences), telemetry
1477	    new_kept = [sv for i, sv in enumerate(kept_sentences) if i not in drop_idx]
1478	    return new_kept, telemetry

1481	async def _fill_one_slot(
1482	    slot: ContractSlotPlan,
1483	    entity_id: str,
1484	    frame_row: FrameRow,
1485	    contract_entity: RequiredEntity,
1486	    research_question: str,
1487	    llm_call: Any,  # async callable(prompt) -> (response_text, in_tok, out_tok)
1488	) -> tuple[SlotFillPayload, int, int]:
1489	    """Produce a SlotFillPayload for one (slot, entity) pair.
1490	
1491	    V31 (M-70) dispatch: regulatory entities (FDA, EMA, NICE, HC)
1492	    route through `regulatory_synthesizer` instead of M-58 contract
1493	    slot extraction. Codex strategic review 2026-04-25: M-58's
1494	    verbatim-substring contract is too rigid for page-scale prose
1495	    synthesis; regulatory entities need PROSE synthesis from
1496	    segmented page sections, not field-level extraction.
1497	
1498	    Three paths for non-regulatory:
1499	      - gap row (FRAME_GAP_UNRECOVERABLE): `compose_gap_payload`, no LLM call.
1500	      - non-gap row, LLM raises (network / timeout):
1501	        `_build_not_extractable_payload`, fail-loud via M-59 FAIL_MIN_FIELDS.
1502	      - non-gap row, parse raises: `_build_not_extractable_payload`.
1503	      - non-gap row, happy path: `parse_slot_fill_response`.
1504	
1505	    Codex M-63 REJECT Blocker 2 fix: non-gap LLM-exception path
1506	    previously called `compose_gap_payload`, which hard-raises on
1507	    non-gap provenance. That turned a planned honest fallback into
1508	    a hard pipeline failure. Now both exception paths route to
1509	    `_build_not_extractable_payload` (see its docstring for rationale).
1510	    """
1511	    required_fields = tuple(contract_entity.required_fields)
1512	
1513	    if frame_row.provenance_class == ProvenanceClass.FRAME_GAP_UNRECOVERABLE:
1514	        payload = compose_gap_payload(slot, frame_row, required_fields)
1515	        return payload, 0, 0
1516	
1517	    # I-run11-010 (#1056, S2): a METADATA_ONLY frame row with an empty/near-empty direct_quote has no
1518	    # verifiable span to cite — emit an honest all-not_extractable payload (surfaced by M-59 as
1519	    # FAIL_MIN_FIELDS / curator-actionable) WITHOUT calling the LLM on an empty span, so strict_verify
1520	    # is not the ONLY thing standing between an empty authoritatively-cited T1 span and a generated
1521	    # claim. NOT compose_gap_payload — that helper HARD-RAISES on non-gap provenance by design (the
1522	    # M-58 symmetric guard); _build_not_extractable_payload is the sanctioned non-gap skip path. A
1523	    # METADATA_ONLY row that DOES carry a usable quote (>= the verifiable-span floor) still extracts.
1524	    if (
1525	        frame_row.provenance_class == ProvenanceClass.METADATA_ONLY
1526	        and len((frame_row.direct_quote or "").strip()) < _MIN_VERIFIABLE_SPAN_CHARS
1527	    ):
1528	        payload = _build_not_extractable_payload(
1529	            slot, frame_row.entity_id, frame_row, required_fields
1530	        )
1531	        return payload, 0, 0
1532	
1533	    # V31 dispatch: regulatory entities go through M-70 prose
1534	    # synthesis instead of M-58 field extraction.
1535	    from .regulatory_synthesizer import (
1536	        is_regulatory_entity,
1537	        build_regulatory_synthesis_prompt,
1538	        parse_regulatory_synthesis_response,
1539	        RegulatorySynthesisError,
1540	        _segment_regulatory_text,
1541	    )
1542	    if is_regulatory_entity(contract_entity):
1543	        segments = _segment_regulatory_text(
1544	            frame_row.direct_quote or "",
1545	            required_fields,
1546	            contract_entity.jurisdiction,
1547	        )
1548	        if not segments:
1549	            # No headings matched — fall back to all not_extractable.
1550	            # M-68 gap-disclosure fallback at the section level
1551	            # will still render the heading + cited gap.
1552	            logger.info(
1553	                "[m70] no regulatory segments matched for "
1554	                "entity_id=%r jurisdiction=%r — degrading to "
1555	                "all not_extractable",
1556	                entity_id, contract_entity.jurisdiction,
1557	            )
1558	            payload = _build_not_extractable_payload(
1559	                slot, entity_id, frame_row, required_fields,
1560	            )
1561	            return payload, 0, 0
1562	        prompt = build_regulatory_synthesis_prompt(
1563	            slot, frame_row, contract_entity, segments,
1564	            research_question,
1565	        )
1566	        try:
1567	            response_text, in_tok, out_tok = await llm_call(prompt)
1568	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
1569	            logger.warning(
1570	                "[m70] LLM call failed for entity_id=%r: %s",
1571	                entity_id, exc,
1572	            )
1573	            payload = _build_not_extractable_payload(
1574	                slot, entity_id, frame_row, required_fields,
1575	            )
1576	            return payload, 0, 0
1577	        try:
1578	            payload = parse_regulatory_synthesis_response(
1579	                response_text, slot, frame_row, required_fields,
1580	                segments,
1581	            )
1582	        except RegulatorySynthesisError as exc:
1583	            logger.warning(
1584	                "[m70] regulatory synthesis parse failed for "
1585	                "entity_id=%r: %s", entity_id, exc,
1586	            )
1587	            payload = _build_not_extractable_payload(
1588	                slot, entity_id, frame_row, required_fields,
1589	            )
1590	            return payload, in_tok, out_tok
1591	        return payload, in_tok, out_tok
1592	
1593	    # Non-regulatory: legacy M-58 path.
1594	    prompt = build_slot_fill_prompt(
1595	        slot, frame_row, required_fields, research_question,
1596	    )
1597	    try:
1598	        response_text, in_tok, out_tok = await llm_call(prompt)
1599	    except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
1600	        logger.warning(
1601	            "[m63] LLM call failed for entity_id=%r slot_id=%r: %s",
1602	            entity_id, slot.slot_id, exc,
1603	        )
1604	        payload = _build_not_extractable_payload(
1605	            slot, entity_id, frame_row, required_fields,
1606	        )
1607	        return payload, 0, 0
1608	
1609	    try:
1610	        payload = parse_slot_fill_response(
1611	            response_text, slot, frame_row, required_fields,
1612	        )
1613	    except SlotFillParseError as exc:
1614	        logger.warning(
1615	            "[m63] slot-fill parse failed for entity_id=%r: %s",
1616	            entity_id, exc,
1617	        )
1618	        payload = _build_not_extractable_payload(
1619	            slot, entity_id, frame_row, required_fields,
1620	        )
1621	        return payload, in_tok, out_tok
1622	
1623	    return payload, in_tok, out_tok
