1626	async def run_contract_section(
1627	    plan: ContractSectionPlanExt,
1628	    evidence_pool: dict[str, dict[str, Any]],
1629	    *,
1630	    llm_call: Any,
1631	    section_result_cls: Any,  # SectionResult class (injected to avoid
1632	                               # circular import)
1633	    strict_verify_fn: Any,     # strict_verify callable (injected)
1634	    rewrite_fn: Any,           # _rewrite_draft_with_spans (injected)
1635	    credibility_analysis: Any = None,  # I-cred-008b (#1162): advisory per-claim disclosure; None => byte-identical
1636	    # I-arch-004 F32 (#1255): adapter for the per-entity NARRATIVE paragraph call.
1637	    # The narrative is PROSE; `llm_call` is the JSON-only contract-slot adapter
1638	    # (system: "JSON only, no prose") used for slot-fill + regulatory synthesis,
1639	    # whose responses are parsed as JSON. Passing prose through the JSON-only
1640	    # system message gave the model conflicting instructions. When None, fall back
1641	    # to `llm_call` so existing callers stay byte-identical. The faithfulness gate
1642	    # (per-sentence verify in the rescue-INELIGIBLE narrative stream) is unchanged.
1643	    narrative_llm_call: Any = None,
1644	    # I-beatboth-003 (#1280): injectable SURE-RAG relevance judge. Threaded into the
1645	    # resolve_provenance_to_citations call below so the gate's per-citation demotion fires on
1646	    # THIS production render path (the V30 contract slot-regroup, which the §-1.4 harness
1647	    # mocks). None (default) => the live GLM-5.2 judge (gate only ON under PG_RELEVANCE_GATE);
1648	    # with the gate OFF the param is inert => byte-identical.
1649	    relevance_judge_fn: Any = None,
1650	) -> tuple[Any, list[SlotFillPayload]]:
1651	    """Run one contract SECTION. Returns (SectionResult,
1652	    list[SlotFillPayload]). The payloads are threaded back to
1653	    M-64 for real M-59 validation at sweep integration time.
1654	
1655	    Legacy-compatible output shape (Codex M-63 REJECT Blocker 3):
1656	
1657	    - `verified_text` has `[N]` numbered-citation markers (NOT raw
1658	      `[#ev:...]` span tokens) so report.md renders as a reader
1659	      expects.
1660	    - `biblio_slice` is a populated list of
1661	      {num, evidence_id, url, tier, statement} dicts so the global
1662	      bibliography merge + per-section [N] remap both work.
1663	    - `### {subsection_title}` headings are re-injected AFTER
1664	      strict_verify and citation resolution, grouped by slot, so
1665	      strict_verify never sees heading prose (it would fail the
1666	      content-word overlap check).
1667	    """
1668	    from .provenance_generator import (
1669	        resolve_provenance_to_citations,
1670	        _strip_bogus_ev_markers,
1671	        _bogus_marker_evidence_id,
1672	        _BOGUS_EV_MARKER_RE,
1673	        _RESOLVE_MIN_CONTENT_WORDS,
1674	        _RESOLVE_MIN_PROSE_CHARS,
1675	        # I-arch-005 B6/B8 (#1257): the keystone basket index helpers the V30 contract
1676	        # slot-regroup feeds into the extracted contract_sentence_citation_nums helper (which
1677	        # itself imports verified_corroborators_for_tokens + the I-beatboth-011 P1#1 claim-local
1678	        # corroborator filter), so the per-sentence attachment decision is IDENTICAL to the
1679	        # legacy resolver's.
1680	        _basket_for_biblio,
1681	        build_basket_supports_by_cluster,
1682	    )
1683	
1684	    # I-arch-004 F32 (#1255): resolve the narrative-paragraph adapter. None => use
1685	    # the JSON-only `llm_call` (byte-identical to the pre-fix behaviour for callers
1686	    # that do not pass a prose adapter). The production caller threads the prose
1687	    # adapter so the narrative is generated under a prose system message.
1688	    _narrative_call = narrative_llm_call if narrative_llm_call is not None else llm_call
1689	
1690	    payloads: list[SlotFillPayload] = []
1691	    total_in_tok = 0
1692	    total_out_tok = 0
1693	    # I-arch-004 F02(c) (#1255): count narrative paragraphs that came back empty so an
1694	    # empty narrative is SURFACED (warned + counted), never silently dropped at the
1695	    # `if narr_text and narr_text.strip()` guard below. Supplementary stream → counted,
1696	    # not fatal (deterministic slot prose remains the primary deliverable).
1697	    _empty_narrative_slots = 0
1698	    all_entity_ids: list[str] = []
1699	
1700	    # entity_id -> slot_id so kept sentences can be regrouped into
1701	    # slot blocks after strict_verify.
1702	    entity_to_slot_id: dict[str, str] = {}
1703	    # slot_id -> subsection_title + preserve order from outline
1704	    slot_order: list[str] = []
1705	    slot_subsection: dict[str, str] = {}
1706	    # slot_id -> primary entity_id (first entity in slot.entity_ids).
1707	    # Used by M-68 Fix #1b (Qwen citation_tightness regression):
1708	    # gap-disclosure prose must carry a citation marker pointing
1709	    # at the bound contract entity so Qwen's citation-tightness
1710	    # rule passes. Without this, run-8 release_allowed=False
1711	    # despite all 15 slots rendering (Structure win → release loss).
1712	    slot_primary_entity: dict[str, str] = {}
1713	
1714	    # Per-slot raw prose blocks (body-only — no headings) so the

content.content:
1	"""V32 — M-71 contradiction-aware hedging for section prose.
2	
3	Codex strategic review (2026-04-25): run-9..run-11 Qwen flagged
4	hedging_appropriateness=needs_revision because the explicit
5	contradiction disclosure lives only in the appendix while the body
6	sections (Safety, Comparative, Population Subgroups) make assertive
7	claims without acknowledging the disagreements.
8	
9	This module:
10	
11	  1. CLASSIFIES contradictions by section relevance using
12	     subject/predicate keyword tagging.
13	  2. FILTERS to high-severity, same-endpoint/same-population
14	     clusters per section so the LLM doesn't get flooded by noisy
15	     detector output.
16	  3. RENDERS a SECTION_HEDGING_BLOCK that gets injected into the
17	     section prompt instructing the LLM to add ONE hedged sentence
18	     when a contradiction materially changes interpretation.
19	
20	Codex's framing (verbatim):
21	  "Run-11 Qwen is mainly objecting to hedging, not to section
22	   layout or missing section types. You already have contradiction
23	   JSON and the relevant body sections. The missing step is
24	   section-local injection: feed only high-severity, same-endpoint/
25	   same-population disagreement clusters into Safety, Comparative,
26	   and Population Subgroups, and require one hedged sentence when
27	   a contradiction materially changes interpretation."
28	
29	## Section relevance taxonomy
30	
31	| Section            | Relevant predicates / subjects                   |
32	|--------------------|--------------------------------------------------|
33	| Safety             | hypoglycemia, adverse events, GI events, AE     |
34	| Comparative        | weight, body weight, HbA1c, vs                  |
35	| Population Subgroups | weight loss, BMI, age, dose                    |
36	| Efficacy           | HbA1c, primary endpoint, ETD                    |
37	
38	A contradiction is relevant to a section iff its
39	subject/predicate matches one of the section's keyword lists.
40	
41	## Severity gate
42	
43	A contradiction is high-severity iff:
44	  - At least 3 distinct values cited (i.e., genuine multi-source
45	    disagreement, not a single outlier)
46	  - Numeric range > 30% relative spread
47	  - Tier mix includes ≥1 T1 source (so the disagreement isn't
48	    pure noise from low-tier outliers)
49	"""
50	from __future__ import annotations
51	
52	import logging
53	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
54	from typing import Any
55	
56	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.contradiction_hedging")
57	
58	
59	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
60	# Section relevance classification
61	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
62	_SECTION_KEYWORDS: dict[str, frozenset[str]] = {
63	    "safety": frozenset({
64	        "hypoglycemia", "adverse", "gi", "gastrointestinal",
65	        "nausea", "vomiting", "diarrhea", "discontinuation",
66	        "tolerability", "ae", "serious",
67	    }),
68	    "comparative": frozenset({
69	        "weight", "body weight", "weight loss", "hba1c",
70	        "ttr", "weight reduction", "vs", "versus",
71	    }),
72	    "population subgroups": frozenset({
73	        "weight loss", "weight reduction", "bmi",
74	        "body mass index", "age", "elderly", "dose",
75	        "ethnicity", "subgroup",
76	    }),
77	    "efficacy": frozenset({
78	        "hba1c", "primary endpoint", "etd",
79	        "treatment difference", "glycemic", "glucose",
80	    }),
81	}
82	
83	
84	def _section_keywords_for(title: str) -> frozenset[str]:
85	    """Look up the section's keyword set by title (case-insensitive,
86	    fuzzy)."""
87	    norm = title.strip().lower()
88	    for key, kws in _SECTION_KEYWORDS.items():
89	        if key in norm:
90	            return kws
91	    return frozenset()
92	
93	
94	def _contradiction_text_blob(c: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
95	    """Concatenate subject + predicate + dose into a search blob."""
96	    parts = []
97	    for k in ("subject", "predicate", "dose", "endpoint", "context"):
98	        v = c.get(k)
99	        if isinstance(v, str):
100	            parts.append(v.lower())
101	    return " ".join(parts)
102	
103	
104	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
105	# Severity gate
106	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
107	def _is_high_severity(c: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
108	    """Return True iff the contradiction is worth surfacing in body
109	    prose. Codex's gate: ≥3 distinct values + ≥30% relative spread
110	    + ≥1 T1 source."""
111	    values = c.get("values") or c.get("cited_values") or []
112	    if not isinstance(values, list) or len(values) < 3:
113	        return False
114	    nums: list[float] = []
115	    for v in values:
116	        if isinstance(v, (int, float)):
117	            nums.append(float(v))
118	        elif isinstance(v, dict):
119	            nv = v.get("value")
120	            if isinstance(nv, (int, float)):
121	                nums.append(float(nv))
122	    if len(nums) < 3:
123	        return False
124	    lo, hi = min(nums), max(nums)
125	    if lo == 0:
126	        # Avoid div-by-zero; require absolute spread > 1
127	        if hi - lo < 1:
128	            return False
129	    else:
130	        rel_spread = abs(hi - lo) / max(abs(lo), abs(hi))
131	        if rel_spread < 0.30:
132	            return False
133	    # Tier mix check
134	    tiers = c.get("tiers") or c.get("source_tiers") or []
135	    if isinstance(tiers, list) and not any(
136	        isinstance(t, str) and t.upper() == "T1" for t in tiers
137	    ):
138	        return False
139	    return True
140	
141	
142	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
143	# Public entrypoint
144	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
145	@dataclass(frozen=True)
146	class SectionContradictionHint:
147	    """One contradiction routed to one section, ready for prompt
148	    injection."""
149	    section_title: str
150	    subject: str
151	    predicate: str
152	    value_range: str  # "5.0 to 93.5%"
153	    tiers: tuple[str, ...]  # ("T1", "T2", "T4")
154	
155	
156	def filter_section_contradictions(
157	    section_title: str,
158	    contradictions: list[dict[str, Any]] | None,
159	    *,
160	    max_per_section: int = 2,
161	) -> list[SectionContradictionHint]:
162	    """Return up to `max_per_section` contradiction hints relevant
163	    to the named section. Filters by:
164	      1. Section keyword overlap with subject+predicate
165	      2. High-severity (Codex gate)
166	      3. Top-N truncation by relative spread (largest first)
167	    """
168	    if not contradictions:
169	        return []
170	    keywords = _section_keywords_for(section_title)
171	    if not keywords:
172	        return []
173	
174	    candidates: list[tuple[float, SectionContradictionHint]] = []
175	    for c in contradictions:
176	        if not isinstance(c, dict):
177	            continue
178	        blob = _contradiction_text_blob(c)
179	        if not any(kw in blob for kw in keywords):
180	            continue
181	        if not _is_high_severity(c):
182	            continue
183	        # Severity score (= relative spread)
184	        values = c.get("values") or c.get("cited_values") or []
185	        nums: list[float] = []
186	        for v in values:
187	            if isinstance(v, (int, float)):
188	                nums.append(float(v))
189	            elif isinstance(v, dict):
190	                nv = v.get("value")
191	                if isinstance(nv, (int, float)):
192	                    nums.append(float(nv))
193	        if len(nums) < 3:
194	            continue
195	        lo, hi = min(nums), max(nums)
196	        spread = abs(hi - lo)
197	
198	        tiers_raw = c.get("tiers") or c.get("source_tiers") or []
199	        tiers = tuple(
200	            t for t in tiers_raw if isinstance(t, str)
201	        )
202	        hint = SectionContradictionHint(
203	            section_title=section_title,
204	            subject=str(c.get("subject", "")),
205	            predicate=str(c.get("predicate", "")),
206	            value_range=f"{lo:g} to {hi:g}",
207	            tiers=tiers,
208	        )
209	        candidates.append((spread, hint))
210	
211	    # Sort by spread descending
212	    candidates.sort(key=lambda x: x[0], reverse=True)
213	    return [h for _, h in candidates[:max_per_section]]
214	
215	
216	def render_section_hedging_block(
217	    hints: list[SectionContradictionHint],
218	) -> str:
219	    """Render a section-local hedging instruction block for the LLM
220	    prompt. Returns empty string when no hints (no prompt change for
221	    that section)."""
222	    if not hints:
223	        return ""
224	    lines = [
225	        "",
226	        "=== M-71 SECTION-LOCAL HEDGING REQUIREMENT ===",
227	        (
228	            "The contradiction detector flagged the following "
229	            "high-severity numeric disagreements in this section's "
230	            "evidence pool. INCLUDE ONE HEDGED SENTENCE acknowledging "
231	            "the disagreement in the body prose (not just the "
232	            "Limitations appendix). Use language like 'sources disagree "
233	            "on the magnitude of', 'reported values range from X to Y', "
234	            "or 'T1 vs T4 evidence diverges on'. Cite the relevant "
235	            "[ev_XXX] markers when stating the range."
236	        ),
237	        "",
238	    ]
239	    for h in hints:
240	        tier_str = "/".join(h.tiers) if h.tiers else "mixed tiers"
241	        lines.append(
242	            f"  - Subject: {h.subject!r}, Predicate: {h.predicate!r}; "
243	            f"reported values range {h.value_range} (source tiers: {tier_str})"
244	        )
245	    lines.append("")
246	    return "\n".join(lines)
247

1	"""I-arch-004 F04 (#539/#629): corpus-snapshot persistence for --resume durability.
2	
3	The honest-sweep pipeline spends the bulk of its wall-clock and money in RETRIEVAL
4	(serper/s2/openalex discovery + fetch + tier-classification) and in the additive
5	deepener/agentic/STORM/saturation merge lanes. A run that is killed mid-GENERATION
6	(the drb_72-class ~473s blank-stream stall, an OOM, an operator Ctrl-C) currently
7	restarts ALL of that retrieval from zero — the F04 checkpoint gap that HARD-gates the
8	full Q1 certification sweep.
9	
10	This module persists the EVIDENCE the generator is about to bill — the fully
11	constructed ``evidence_for_gen`` rows plus the ``retrieval`` corpus (classified
12	sources + evidence rows + the manifest-relevant retrieval counts) — at the single
13	pre-generation seam, AFTER selection + the saturation loop + every contract/upload
14	prepend, so a ``--resume`` reload skips retrieval entirely and re-enters at generation.
15	
16	HARD INVARIANT (CLAUDE.md §-1.3, ABSOLUTE — enforced by design here):
17	    A checkpoint carries DATA, NEVER A VERDICT.
18	    The snapshot stores ONLY retrieved/selected EVIDENCE ROWS (dict data) and the
19	    retrieval COUNTS used for the manifest envelope. It stores NO faithfulness
20	    verdict, NO strict_verify result, NO NLI/4-role/D8 decision, NO "verified" flag.
21	    On ``--resume`` the caller reloads this DATA and RE-RUNS strict_verify / NLI /
22	    4-role / D8 on it from scratch — exactly as a fresh run does. Restoring a cached
23	    faithfulness verdict would be a RELAXATION of the only hard gate and is an
24	    auto-P0; this module makes that impossible by never serializing a verdict.
25	
26	The snapshot is a plain JSON document (schema_version pinned) so it is human-
27	auditable and forward/backward inspectable. ``CorpusSource`` is a flat dataclass
28	(``asdict`` / ``CorpusSource(**d)`` round-trip); ``evidence_rows`` / ``evidence_for_gen``
29	are already ``list[dict]``. No pickle, no code execution on load.
30	"""
31	
32	from __future__ import annotations
33	
34	import json
35	import os
36	from dataclasses import asdict
37	from pathlib import Path
38	from typing import Any
39	
40	# Bump on any incompatible schema change. A reload that sees a different version
41	# FAILS LOUD (refuses to resume) rather than silently feeding a stale-shaped corpus
42	# to the generator — LAW II no-silent-downgrade.
43	SNAPSHOT_SCHEMA_VERSION = 1
44	
45	# The canonical snapshot filename inside a per-query run_dir.
46	SNAPSHOT_FILENAME = "corpus_snapshot.json"
47	
48	# Stage pointer values. Only ``pre_generation`` is persisted today (the F04 spine
49	# seam); the constant is named so a future mid-generation stage pointer is explicit.
50	STAGE_PRE_GENERATION = "pre_generation"
51	
52	
53	def snapshot_path(run_dir: Path) -> Path:
54	    """Deterministic snapshot location for a per-query run_dir."""
55	    return Path(run_dir) / SNAPSHOT_FILENAME
56	
57	
58	def _retrieval_payload(retrieval: Any) -> dict[str, Any]:
59	    """Serialize the retrieval fields the resume path + manifest envelope need.
60	
61	    DATA ONLY — counts + the two evidence collections. ``classified_sources`` is a
62	    list of flat ``CorpusSource`` dataclasses (``asdict``-safe); ``evidence_rows`` is
63	    already ``list[dict]``. Every count is read with a default so a pre-#958 / test
64	    retrieval object round-trips. NO verdict, NO gate result.
65	    """
66	    # I-wire-001 W2 (#1311) P1-1: serialize CorpusSource via corpus_asdict so the
67	    # W2 content-relevance keys are OMITTED at default => the corpus snapshot is
68	    # byte-identical when W2 OFF; a W2-ON demoted source keeps its weight+label so
69	    # a resume run preserves the demotion.
70	    from src.polaris_graph.nodes.corpus_approval_gate import corpus_asdict
71	    return {
72	        "classified_sources": [corpus_asdict(s) for s in getattr(retrieval, "classified_sources", []) or []],
73	        "evidence_rows": list(getattr(retrieval, "evidence_rows", []) or []),
74	        "notes": list(getattr(retrieval, "notes", []) or []),
75	        "counts": {
76	            "total_candidates_pre_filter": getattr(retrieval, "total_candidates_pre_filter", 0),
77	            "candidates_kept_by_scope": getattr(retrieval, "candidates_kept_by_scope", 0),
78	            "candidates_kept_by_offtopic": getattr(retrieval, "candidates_kept_by_offtopic", 0),
79	            "candidates_fetched": getattr(retrieval, "candidates_fetched", 0),
80	            "candidates_failed_fetch": getattr(retrieval, "candidates_failed_fetch", 0),
81	            "candidates_total": getattr(retrieval, "candidates_total", 0),
82	            "candidates_processed": getattr(retrieval, "candidates_processed", 0),
83	            "extraction_finding_rows": getattr(retrieval, "extraction_finding_rows", 0),
84	            "corpus_truncated": bool(getattr(retrieval, "corpus_truncated", False)),
85	        },
86	        "api_calls": dict(getattr(retrieval, "api_calls", {}) or {}),
87	    }
88	
89	
90	def save_corpus_snapshot(
91	    run_dir: Path,
92	    *,
93	    run_id: str,
94	    question: str,
95	    slug: str,
96	    domain: str,
97	    evidence_for_gen: list[dict[str, Any]],
98	    retrieval: Any,
99	    section_drafts: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
100	    stage: str = STAGE_PRE_GENERATION,
101	) -> Path:
102	    """Persist the pre-generation corpus snapshot. Returns the written path.
103	
104	    Atomic write (temp + os.replace) so a kill DURING the snapshot write never
105	    leaves a truncated/half-parsed file that a later --resume would choke on.
106	
107	    ``section_drafts`` is the optional per-section draft layer (raw generator prose
108	    keyed by section heading). It carries DRAFT PROSE ONLY — never a verified flag.
109	    On resume the caller re-runs strict_verify on every reloaded draft; the snapshot
110	    cannot smuggle a cached verdict because none is stored.
111	    """
112	    run_dir = Path(run_dir)
113	    payload: dict[str, Any] = {
114	        "schema_version": SNAPSHOT_SCHEMA_VERSION,
115	        "stage": stage,
116	        "run_id": run_id,
117	        "question": question,
118	        "slug": slug,
119	        "domain": domain,
120	        # DATA: the rows the generator is about to bill (post selection + prepends).
121	        "evidence_for_gen": list(evidence_for_gen or []),
122	        # DATA: the retrieval corpus + counts for manifest reconstruction on resume.
123	        "retrieval": _retrieval_payload(retrieval),
124	        # DRAFT PROSE ONLY (re-verified on resume); default empty.
125	        "section_drafts": dict(section_drafts or {}),
126	    }
127	    path = snapshot_path(run_dir)
128	    tmp = path.with_suffix(path.suffix + ".tmp")
129	    tmp.write_text(
130	        json.dumps(payload, indent=2, sort_keys=True, default=str) + "\n",
131	        encoding="utf-8",
132	    )
133	    os.replace(tmp, path)
134	    return path
135	
136	
137	class CorpusSnapshotError(RuntimeError):
138	    """Raised when a --resume reload cannot trust the on-disk snapshot.
139	
140	    FAIL LOUD (LAW II): a missing/corrupt/version-mismatched snapshot must NOT
141	    silently fall back to a fresh retrieval under --resume (the operator asked to
142	    resume an interrupted run; a silent restart would re-bill retrieval and mask the
143	    interruption). The caller surfaces this as a clean abort.
144	    """
145	
146	
147	def load_corpus_snapshot(run_dir: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
148	    """Reload + validate the corpus snapshot for --resume. Returns the parsed payload.
149	
150	    Raises CorpusSnapshotError on absent / malformed / version-mismatched / empty-
151	    corpus snapshots so the caller fails loud instead of resuming on bad data.
152	    Returns DATA ONLY — the caller MUST re-run every faithfulness gate on it.
153	    """
154	    path = snapshot_path(run_dir)
155	    if not path.exists():
156	        raise CorpusSnapshotError(
157	            f"--resume: no corpus snapshot at {path} (nothing to resume; run without "
158	            f"--resume for a fresh run)"
159	        )
160	    try:
161	        payload = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
162	    except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError) as exc:
163	        raise CorpusSnapshotError(
164	            f"--resume: corpus snapshot at {path} is unreadable/corrupt: {exc}"
165	        ) from exc
166	    if not isinstance(payload, dict):
167	        raise CorpusSnapshotError(f"--resume: corpus snapshot at {path} is not a JSON object")
168	    version = payload.get("schema_version")
169	    if version != SNAPSHOT_SCHEMA_VERSION:
170	        raise CorpusSnapshotError(
171	            f"--resume: corpus snapshot schema_version {version!r} != expected "
172	            f"{SNAPSHOT_SCHEMA_VERSION} at {path}; refusing to resume on a stale-shaped "
173	            f"corpus (re-run fresh)"
174	        )
175	    if not payload.get("evidence_for_gen"):
176	        raise CorpusSnapshotError(
177	            f"--resume: corpus snapshot at {path} has an empty evidence_for_gen; refusing "
178	            f"to resume a run with no generator corpus"
179	        )
180	    return payload
181	
182	
183	def reconstruct_retrieval(payload: dict[str, Any]) -> Any:
184	    """Rebuild a lightweight LiveRetrievalResult from a reloaded snapshot payload.
185	
186	    The reconstructed object carries the same ``classified_sources`` (rehydrated
187	    ``CorpusSource`` dataclasses), ``evidence_rows``, ``notes``, and retrieval counts
188	    the live run had at the pre-generation seam, so the downstream manifest envelope
189	    + adequacy/distribution re-derivation see an identical corpus. It carries NO
190	    verdict — gates re-run on the rows.
191	    """
192	    # Lazy imports keep this module import-light and avoid a cycle through the
193	    # retrieval package at module load.
194	    from src.polaris_graph.nodes.corpus_approval_gate import CorpusSource
195	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.live_retriever import LiveRetrievalResult
196	
197	    retr = payload.get("retrieval") or {}
198	    counts = retr.get("counts") or {}
199	    sources = [
200	        CorpusSource(**{k: v for k, v in (row or {}).items() if k in CorpusSource.__dataclass_fields__})
201	        for row in (retr.get("classified_sources") or [])
202	    ]
203	    return LiveRetrievalResult(
204	        classified_sources=sources,
205	        evidence_rows=list(retr.get("evidence_rows") or []),
206	        total_candidates_pre_filter=int(counts.get("total_candidates_pre_filter", 0) or 0),
207	        candidates_kept_by_scope=int(counts.get("candidates_kept_by_scope", 0) or 0),
208	        candidates_kept_by_offtopic=int(counts.get("candidates_kept_by_offtopic", 0) or 0),
209	        candidates_fetched=int(counts.get("candidates_fetched", 0) or 0),
210	        candidates_failed_fetch=int(counts.get("candidates_failed_fetch", 0) or 0),
211	        api_calls=dict(retr.get("api_calls") or {}),
212	        notes=list(retr.get("notes") or []),
213	        corpus_truncated=bool(counts.get("corpus_truncated", False)),
214	        candidates_total=int(counts.get("candidates_total", 0) or 0),
215	        candidates_processed=int(counts.get("candidates_processed", 0) or 0),
216	        extraction_finding_rows=int(counts.get("extraction_finding_rows", 0) or 0),
217	    )
218

1	"""V34 — M-14 cross-jurisdiction synthesizer (Phase C).
2	
3	Per FINAL_PLAN.md Phase C deliverable #1: close the LB Regulatory
4	gap by surfacing FDA / EMA / MHRA / PMDA / NICE / HC divergence
5	or convergence on label/indication/post-marketing fields.
6	
7	This module layers ON TOP of M-70 regulatory_synthesizer. M-70
8	extracts per-jurisdiction prose for a single regulatory entity
9	(one FDA label, one EMA EPAR, etc). M-14 takes a SET of such
10	per-jurisdiction extractions and emits cross-jurisdiction
11	paragraphs that explicitly call out which jurisdictions agree
12	and which diverge.
13	
14	## LAW II safeguard
15	
16	The dominant Phase C risk for V34 is over-claim — the easy
17	failure mode is to flatten genuine divergence into bland
18	consensus prose ("Regulators worldwide approved drug X for
19	condition Y") that misrepresents reality.
20	
21	The safeguard:
22	  - Every emitted sentence carries inline jurisdiction tags
23	    (e.g., "FDA, EMA approved..." not "Regulators approved...").
24	  - Every emitted sentence carries inline evidence citations
25	    bound to the SOURCE-jurisdiction-tagged evidence_id.
26	  - When jurisdictions disagree, the synthesizer emits a
27	    DIVERGENCE paragraph naming each jurisdiction's position
28	    + citation, NOT a single consensus paragraph.
29	
30	## Pipeline
31	
32	1. INPUT: a list of `JurisdictionFinding` records (one per
33	   jurisdiction × field), each carrying:
34	     - jurisdiction (FDA/EMA/MHRA/PMDA/NICE/HC)
35	     - field_name ("indications", "boxed_warning", ...)
36	     - value (the M-70-synthesized prose for this field)
37	     - bound_ev_id (source evidence id)
38	
39	2. GROUP BY field_name. For each field group:
40	     - If only ONE jurisdiction has a non-empty value, emit a
41	       single-jurisdiction paragraph (no divergence claim).
42	     - If MULTIPLE jurisdictions have values:
43	         - Score pairwise similarity (token Jaccard, lowercase,
44	           stopword-filtered) to detect convergence vs divergence.
45	         - If similarity above threshold across all pairs, emit
46	           a CONVERGENCE paragraph naming all jurisdictions and
47	           binding all citations.
48	         - Otherwise, emit a DIVERGENCE paragraph: one sentence
49	           per jurisdiction, each with its own citation, prefixed
50	           by the divergence-flag clause.
51	
52	3. OUTPUT: rendered Markdown block + a structured payload
53	   capturing the divergence/convergence verdict per field for
54	   downstream consumers (Inspector, audit bundle).
55	
56	## Determinism
57	
58	The synthesizer is fully deterministic: no LLM calls. All prose
59	is templated from the JurisdictionFinding records. This is
60	deliberate — Phase C V34's job is to DETECT divergence, not to
61	generate flowery prose. M-70's LLM-synthesized per-jurisdiction
62	prose is reused verbatim inside M-14's templated paragraphs.
63	
64	## Why a separate module from M-70
65	
66	M-70 operates on ONE jurisdiction at a time. M-14 operates on
67	a SET of M-70 outputs. Splitting keeps M-70's LLM-bound
68	extraction concerns separate from M-14's deterministic
69	divergence-detection concerns.
70	"""
71	
72	from __future__ import annotations
73	
74	import re
75	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
76	from typing import Any
77	
78	
79	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
80	# Canonical jurisdiction enum (string-typed for JSON friendliness)
81	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
82	
83	
84	_KNOWN_JURISDICTIONS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
85	    "FDA",   # United States
86	    "EMA",   # European Union
87	    "MHRA",  # United Kingdom
88	    "PMDA",  # Japan
89	    "NICE",  # UK NICE technology appraisals
90	    "HC",    # Health Canada
91	    "TGA",   # Australia
92	})
93	
94	
95	def is_known_jurisdiction(j: str) -> bool:
96	    """True if the string is a recognized regulatory jurisdiction."""
97	    return j.upper() in _KNOWN_JURISDICTIONS
98	
99	
100	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
101	# Records
102	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
103	
104	
105	@dataclass(frozen=True)
106	class JurisdictionFinding:
107	    """One per-jurisdiction × field extraction (M-70 output projected
108	    into a flatter shape for M-14 consumption).
109	
110	    Attributes:
111	      jurisdiction: One of _KNOWN_JURISDICTIONS, uppercase.
112	      field_name: Canonical field key (e.g. "indications",
113	                  "boxed_warning"). Compared case-insensitively
114	                  across jurisdictions.
115	      value: The M-70-synthesized prose for this field. Empty
116	             string means M-70 emitted not_extractable; M-14
117	             treats that as "no finding" rather than "explicit
118	             absence".
119	      bound_ev_id: Evidence id binding this finding to its source.
120	      source_url: Optional source URL for renderer link-out.
121	    """
122	
123	    jurisdiction: str
124	    field_name: str
125	    value: str
126	    bound_ev_id: str
127	    source_url: str | None = None
128	
129	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
130	        # Normalize jurisdiction case so callers don't have to.
131	        # Frozen dataclass: use object.__setattr__.
132	        object.__setattr__(self, "jurisdiction", self.jurisdiction.upper())
133	
134	
135	@dataclass(frozen=True)
136	class FieldVerdict:
137	    """Cross-jurisdiction verdict for one field across multiple
138	    jurisdictions. Used by Inspector and audit bundle.
139	
140	    Attributes:
141	      field_name: Canonical field key.
142	      verdict: One of "convergence" / "divergence" / "single_source"
143	               / "no_findings".
144	      jurisdictions: Tuple of jurisdiction strings represented in
145	                     this verdict.
146	      bound_ev_ids: Tuple of evidence ids cited (one per
147	                    jurisdiction). Same order as `jurisdictions`.
148	      similarity: Pairwise minimum Jaccard similarity across
149	                  jurisdiction values. 1.0 if only one source.
150	    """
151	
152	    field_name: str
153	    verdict: str
154	    jurisdictions: tuple[str, ...]
155	    bound_ev_ids: tuple[str, ...]
156	    similarity: float
157	
158	
159	@dataclass(frozen=True)
160	class CrossJurisdictionSynthesis:
161	    """Top-level output of `synthesize_cross_jurisdiction()`."""
162	
163	    paragraphs: tuple[str, ...]
164	    verdicts: tuple[FieldVerdict, ...]
165	
166	
167	def synthesis_to_dict(s: CrossJurisdictionSynthesis) -> dict[str, Any]:
168	    return {
169	        "paragraphs": list(s.paragraphs),
170	        "verdicts": [
171	            {
172	                "field_name": v.field_name,
173	                "verdict": v.verdict,
174	                "jurisdictions": list(v.jurisdictions),
175	                "bound_ev_ids": list(v.bound_ev_ids),
176	                "similarity": v.similarity,
177	            }
178	            for v in s.verdicts
179	        ],
180	    }
181	
182	
183	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
184	# Tokenization + similarity (mirrors M-10 template_classifier patterns)
185	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
186	
187	
188	_TOKEN_RE = re.compile(r"[a-z0-9]+")
189	
190	# Stopwords identical to template_classifier so ranking stays
191	# consistent across the codebase. This list is small + conservative
192	# (English question scaffold + clinical filler verbs); it is NOT a
193	# medical NER stoplist.
194	_STOPWORDS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
195	    "a", "an", "the",
196	    "and", "or", "but",
197	    "of", "in", "on", "at", "to", "for", "from", "with", "by",
198	    "is", "are", "was", "were", "be", "been", "being",
199	    "this", "that", "these", "those",
200	    "as", "if", "than", "then", "so", "such",
201	    "use", "used", "using", "uses",
202	    "given", "taking", "received", "receiving",
203	})
204	
205	
206	def _expand_contractions(text: str) -> str:
207	    """Codex M-14 v3 review fix: expand English contractions
208	    BEFORE the _TOKEN_RE splits on apostrophes. v2 tokenized
209	    "isn't approved" as {"isn", "approved"} — fragments — and
210	    the negation guard never saw "not". Expansion via word-
211	    boundary regex on lowercased text.
212	
213	    Codex M-14 v4 review fix: also normalize Unicode smart
214	    apostrophes (U+2018 left, U+2019 right) to ASCII apostrophe
215	    BEFORE the contraction map runs. PDF / Word copy-paste
216	    routinely produces "isn’t" which v3 didn't recognize.
217	    """
218	    if not text:
219	        return text
220	    # Normalize smart quotes first.
221	    out = text.replace("‘", "'").replace("’", "'")
222	    out = out.lower()
223	    # Word-boundary replace each contraction. We iterate the
224	    # static dict in length-DESC order so longer keys
225	    # (e.g. "couldn't") match before shorter ones.
226	    for raw in sorted(_CONTRACTIONS, key=len, reverse=True):
227	        # Use re.sub so word boundaries are respected.
228	        # Apostrophe is not a word char, so \b at the start
229	        # works for the apostrophe-style key; for non-apostrophe
230	        # keys (cant, dont) standard \b applies.
231	        if "'" in raw:
232	            # Match the contraction literally (apostrophe-aware).
233	            pattern = re.compile(r"\b" + re.escape(raw))
234	        else:
235	            pattern = re.compile(r"\b" + re.escape(raw) + r"\b")
236	        out = pattern.sub(_CONTRACTIONS[raw], out)
237	    return out
238	
239	
240	def _tokens(text: str) -> frozenset[str]:
241	    """Lowercased, stopword-filtered content tokens.
242	
243	    Codex M-14 v3 review fix: contractions expanded BEFORE
244	    tokenization so "isn't" → "is not" → contributes "not" to
245	    the token bag.
246	    """
247	    if not text:
248	        return frozenset()
249	    expanded = _expand_contractions(text)
250	    raw = _TOKEN_RE.findall(expanded)
251	    return frozenset(t for t in raw if t not in _STOPWORDS and len(t) > 1)
252	
253	
254	def _jaccard(a: frozenset[str], b: frozenset[str]) -> float:
255	    if not a or not b:
256	        return 0.0
257	    inter = a & b
258	    union = a | b
259	    if not union:
260	        return 0.0
261	    return len(inter) / len(union)
262	
263	
264	# Codex M-14 v2 review fix: convergence threshold raised from 0.5
265	# to 0.7. v1 default of 0.5 let direct negations cross the floor
266	# ("approved for adults" vs "not approved for adults" → Jaccard
267	# 0.667 → falsely convergence). 0.7 alone is not enough — see
268	# the force-divergence qualifier guards below — but it's a
269	# necessary tightening.
270	_DEFAULT_CONVERGENCE_FLOOR = 0.7
271	
272	
273	# Codex M-14 v2 review fix: hard force-divergence qualifier guards.
274	# Token-set Jaccard alone treats "X" and "not X" as 0.667 similar,
275	# which is the exact LAW II over-claim failure Phase C is trying
276	# to prevent. These guards force DIVERGENCE when ANY pair of
277	# values differs on a qualifier that flips the meaning, BEFORE
278	# Jaccard is even consulted.
279	#
280	# Each guard is a tuple of (token_set, name). If one value
281	# contains any token from the set and another value does NOT,
282	# the guard fires.
283	
284	# Negation tokens: their presence vs absence flips meaning
285	# entirely. "approved" vs "not approved" must never converge.
286	# Codex M-14 v3 review fix: contractions need an EXPANSION step
287	# before tokenization. v2 tokenized "isn't approved" as
288	# {"isn","approved"} (the apostrophe split the contraction into
289	# fragments) and the negation guard never saw "not". This map
290	# normalizes contractions to their expanded form BEFORE _tokens()
291	# runs, so "isn't" / "aren't" / "can't" / "won't" etc. all
292	# contribute "not" to the token bag and trigger the negation
293	# guard.
294	_CONTRACTIONS: dict[str, str] = {
295	    "isn't": "is not", "isnt": "is not",
296	    "aren't": "are not", "arent": "are not",
297	    "wasn't": "was not", "wasnt": "was not",
298	    "weren't": "were not", "werent": "were not",
299	    "can't": "can not", "cant": "can not",
300	    "cannot": "can not",
301	    "couldn't": "could not", "couldnt": "could not",
302	    "won't": "will not", "wont": "will not",
303	    "wouldn't": "would not", "wouldnt": "would not",
304	    "shouldn't": "should not", "shouldnt": "should not",
305	    "doesn't": "does not", "doesnt": "does not",
306	    "don't": "do not", "dont": "do not",
307	    "didn't": "did not", "didnt": "did not",
308	    "hasn't": "has not", "hasnt": "has not",
309	    "haven't": "have not", "havent": "have not",
310	    "hadn't": "had not", "hadnt": "had not",
311	    "shan't": "shall not", "shant": "shall not",
312	    "mustn't": "must not", "mustnt": "must not",
313	    "needn't": "need not", "neednt": "need not",
314	}
315	
316	
317	_NEGATION_TOKENS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
318	    "not", "no", "never", "without",
319	    "contraindicated", "withheld", "denied", "rejected",
320	    "withdrawn", "suspended",
321	    # Codex M-14 v3 review fix: also catch refusal verbs that
322	    # don't contain "not" but flip meaning regardless.
323	    "refused", "revoked", "negative",
324	})
325	
326	# Status-pending tokens: regulatory positions still in flux
327	# don't converge with finalized positions, even if the wording
328	# overlaps.
329	_PENDING_TOKENS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
330	    "pending", "review", "ongoing", "preliminary",
331	    "interim", "provisional",
332	})
333	
334	# Scope-limiter tokens: "only", "exclusively", "restricted to"
335	# materially change the indicated population. Their presence vs
336	# absence is a divergence signal.
337	_SCOPE_LIMITER_TOKENS: frozenset[str] = frozenset({
338	    "only", "exclusively", "restricted",
339	    "limited",  # "limited to adults" vs "all adults"
340	})
341	
342	
343	def _force_divergence(values: list[str]) -> bool:
344	    """Return True if the qualifier guards detect a hard
345	    divergence between any pair of values.
346	
347	    Codex M-14 v2 review fix: applied BEFORE Jaccard so that
348	    negation/scope/pending mismatches force DIVERGENCE regardless
349	    of token overlap.
350	    """
351	    if len(values) < 2:
352	        return False
353	    for guard in (_NEGATION_TOKENS, _PENDING_TOKENS, _SCOPE_LIMITER_TOKENS):
354	        flags = [bool(_tokens(v) & guard) for v in values]
355	        # If ANY value has a guard token and ANY OTHER value does
356	        # NOT, the guard fires.
357	        if any(flags) and not all(flags):
358	            return True
359	    # Codex M-14 v2: numeric mismatch guard. Different numeric
360	    # values in the prose (dose mg, age cutoffs, percentages) flip
361	    # meaning even if the surrounding tokens overlap.
362	    numerics = [_extract_numeric_tokens(v) for v in values]
363	    if any(numerics):
364	        # If two values both have numerics and the sets disagree
365	        # at all, force divergence.
366	        nonempty = [n for n in numerics if n]
367	        if len(nonempty) >= 2:
368	            for i in range(len(nonempty)):
369	                for j in range(i + 1, len(nonempty)):
370	                    if nonempty[i] != nonempty[j]:
371	                        return True
372	        # Asymmetric: one value has numerics, another doesn't.
373	        if any(n for n in numerics) and any(not n for n in numerics):
374	            return True
375	    return False
376	
377	
378	# Codex M-14 v3 review fix: extended regex to consume thousands-
379	# separator commas so "1,000 mg" and "1000 mg" produce the same
380	# numeric token. v2 regex `\b\d+(?:\.\d+)?\b` saw "1,000" as
381	# {"1", "000"} and falsely diverged from {"1000"}.
382	_NUMERIC_RE = re.compile(r"\b\d{1,3}(?:,\d{3})+(?:\.\d+)?\b|\b\d+(?:\.\d+)?\b")
383	
384	
385	def _extract_numeric_tokens(text: str) -> frozenset[str]:
386	    """Extract bare numeric tokens (integers, decimals) from text.
387	    Used by the numeric-mismatch divergence guard.
388	
389	    Codex M-14 v3 review fix: thousands-separator commas are
390	    stripped from the matched token so "1,000" and "1000"
391	    normalize to the same canonical form.
392	    """
393	    if not text:
394	        return frozenset()
395	    raw = _NUMERIC_RE.findall(text)
396	    return frozenset(s.replace(",", "") for s in raw)
397	
398	
399	# Codex M-14 v2 review fix: divergence-flattening prose guard.
400	# Even on the divergence path (where bullets list per-jurisdiction
401	# values verbatim), if an upstream M-70 prose contains language
402	# that itself flattens jurisdictions ("regulators worldwide", etc.)
403	# the bullet would silently preserve it. Detect + neutralize.
404	#
405	# Codex M-14 v3 review fix: v2 was exact-substring match. Variants
406	# like "approved worldwide", "approved globally", "internationally
407	# approved", "consensus across jurisdictions" bypassed it. v3
408	# uses regex with `\b` word boundaries and matches the trigger
409	# WORDS (worldwide, globally, internationally, consensus,
410	# unanimous, international) rather than fixed phrases — any prose
411	# containing those flattening signals gets neutralized regardless
412	# of surrounding word order.
413	_FLATTENING_TRIGGERS: tuple[str, ...] = (
414	    "worldwide",
415	    "globally",
416	    "internationally",
417	    "international consensus",
418	    "global consensus",
419	    "consensus across jurisdictions",
420	    "unanimous",
421	    "unanimously",  # adverb form — regex \b requires explicit listing
422	    "all regulators",
423	    "all jurisdictions",
424	    "every jurisdiction",
425	    "every regulator",
426	    "regulators worldwide",
427	    "regulators globally",
428	)
429	
430	# Build a single regex that matches any trigger as a word boundary.
431	_FLATTENING_RE = re.compile(
432	    r"\b(?:" + "|".join(re.escape(t) for t in _FLATTENING_TRIGGERS) + r")\b",
433	    flags=re.IGNORECASE,
434	)
435	
436	
437	def _strip_flattening_phrases(text: str) -> str:
438	    """Replace flattening triggers with explicit single-
439	    jurisdiction framing so the divergence path can never
440	    preserve smuggled consensus language inside a bullet.
441	
442	    Codex M-14 v3 review fix: regex with word boundaries replaces
443	    the v2 exact-substring match. Catches "approved worldwide",
444	    "approved globally", "internationally approved", "unanimously
445	    approved", "consensus across jurisdictions", etc.
446	    """
447	    if not text:
448	        return text
449	    return _FLATTENING_RE.sub("[this jurisdiction]", text)
450	
451	
452	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
453	# Synthesis
454	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
455	
456	
457	def _format_field_label(field_name: str) -> str:
458	    """`indications` → 'Indications'; `boxed_warning` → 'Boxed warning'."""
459	    label = field_name.replace("_", " ")
460	    return label[:1].upper() + label[1:] if label else label
461	
462	
463	def _join_jurisdictions(juris: list[str]) -> str:
464	    """Format a list of jurisdiction names as 'A, B, and C'."""
465	    if not juris:
466	        return ""
467	    if len(juris) == 1:
468	        return juris[0]
469	    if len(juris) == 2:
470	        return f"{juris[0]} and {juris[1]}"
471	    return f"{', '.join(juris[:-1])}, and {juris[-1]}"
472	
473	
474	def _emit_single_source_paragraph(
475	    field_name: str,
476	    finding: JurisdictionFinding,
477	) -> str:
478	    """When only one jurisdiction has a value, emit a single-source
479	    paragraph that explicitly names the jurisdiction (not a generic
480	    'regulators agree' claim).
481	
482	    Codex M-14 v2 review fix: citation rendered as `[cite:ev_id]`
483	    (renderer-only contract distinct from the V30 strict_verify
484	    `[#ev:id:start-end]` token). This module emits cross-
485	    jurisdiction synthesis prose; per-sentence span-bound
486	    provenance is the responsibility of the upstream M-70 prose,
487	    which is reused verbatim here. Renderers that need
488	    machine-readable citation IDs use the FieldVerdict.bound_ev_ids
489	    field, not regex parsing of the prose.
490	    """
491	    label = _format_field_label(field_name)
492	    value = _strip_flattening_phrases(finding.value).rstrip(".").strip()
493	    return (
494	        f"**{label} ({finding.jurisdiction} only).** {value} "
495	        f"[cite:{finding.bound_ev_id}]. Other jurisdictions in scope did not "
496	        f"surface a {field_name.replace('_', ' ')} finding for this entity."
497	    )
498	
499	
500	def _emit_convergence_paragraph(
501	    field_name: str,
502	    findings: list[JurisdictionFinding],
503	) -> str:
504	    """All jurisdictions surface substantively similar prose AND
505	    pass the qualifier guards. Emit a CONVERGENCE paragraph that
506	    lists every contributing jurisdiction by name and binds every
507	    citation.
508	
509	    Codex M-14 v2 review fix: deterministic canonical selection
510	    via (length DESC, jurisdiction ASC) sort key. Pure max-by-len
511	    was order-sensitive when two values had the same length.
512	    Citation order is also (jurisdiction ASC).
513	    """
514	    label = _format_field_label(field_name)
515	    juris = sorted(f.jurisdiction for f in findings)
516	    juris_clause = _join_jurisdictions(juris)
517	    # Canonical = longest value; ties broken by jurisdiction name
518	    # (alphabetical) for determinism across input permutations.
519	    canonical = sorted(
520	        findings, key=lambda f: (-len(f.value), f.jurisdiction),
521	    )[0]
522	    # Sanitize the canonical prose so smuggled flattening language
523	    # inside an upstream M-70 value can't reach the renderer.
524	    value = _strip_flattening_phrases(canonical.value).rstrip(".").strip()
525	    # Citations sorted by jurisdiction so the order is invariant
526	    # under input permutation.
527	    cite_tokens = [
528	        f"[cite:{f.bound_ev_id}]"
529	        for f in sorted(findings, key=lambda x: x.jurisdiction)
530	    ]
531	    citations = " ".join(cite_tokens)
532	    return (
533	        f"**{label} (convergence: {juris_clause}).** {value}. "
534	        f"Citations: {citations}."
535	    )
536	
537	
538	def _emit_divergence_paragraph(
539	    field_name: str,
540	    findings: list[JurisdictionFinding],
541	) -> str:
542	    """Jurisdictions surface materially different prose. Emit a
543	    DIVERGENCE paragraph: one bullet per jurisdiction, each with
544	    its own citation. The opening clause flags divergence
545	    explicitly so renderers can highlight it.
546	
547	    Codex M-14 v2 review fix: bullet bodies pass through
548	    `_strip_flattening_phrases` so even if upstream M-70 prose
549	    smuggled in "regulators worldwide" language, it gets
550	    neutralized before rendering.
551	    """
552	    label = _format_field_label(field_name)
553	    juris = sorted(f.jurisdiction for f in findings)
554	    juris_clause = _join_jurisdictions(juris)
555	    bullets = []
556	    for f in sorted(findings, key=lambda x: x.jurisdiction):
557	        value = _strip_flattening_phrases(f.value).rstrip(".").strip()
558	        bullets.append(
559	            f"- **{f.jurisdiction}.** {value} [cite:{f.bound_ev_id}]."
560	        )
561	    bullet_block = "\n".join(bullets)
562	    return (
563	        f"**{label} (divergence across {juris_clause}).** Regulatory "
564	        f"positions differ on this field. Per-jurisdiction findings:\n"
565	        f"{bullet_block}"
566	    )
567	
568	
569	def _verdict_for_field(
570	    field_name: str,
571	    findings: list[JurisdictionFinding],
572	    convergence_floor: float,
573	) -> tuple[FieldVerdict, str]:
574	    """Compute the verdict + paragraph for a single field group."""
575	    nonempty = [f for f in findings if f.value.strip()]
576	    if not nonempty:
577	        verdict = FieldVerdict(
578	            field_name=field_name,
579	            verdict="no_findings",
580	            jurisdictions=(),
581	            bound_ev_ids=(),
582	            similarity=0.0,
583	        )
584	        return verdict, ""
585	
586	    if len(nonempty) == 1:
587	        f = nonempty[0]
588	        verdict = FieldVerdict(
589	            field_name=field_name,
590	            verdict="single_source",
591	            jurisdictions=(f.jurisdiction,),
592	            bound_ev_ids=(f.bound_ev_id,),
593	            similarity=1.0,
594	        )
595	        return verdict, _emit_single_source_paragraph(field_name, f)
596	
597	    # Multi-jurisdiction case — compute pairwise minimum Jaccard.
598	    tokens_per = [(f, _tokens(f.value)) for f in nonempty]
599	    min_sim = 1.0
600	    for i in range(len(tokens_per)):
601	        for j in range(i + 1, len(tokens_per)):
602	            s = _jaccard(tokens_per[i][1], tokens_per[j][1])
603	            if s < min_sim:
604	                min_sim = s
605	
606	    juris_sorted = sorted(f.jurisdiction for f in nonempty)
607	    ev_ids_sorted = tuple(
608	        f.bound_ev_id for f in sorted(nonempty, key=lambda x: x.jurisdiction)
609	    )
610	
611	    # Codex M-14 v2 review fix: hard force-divergence guards run
612	    # BEFORE the Jaccard threshold check. Negation/scope-limiter/
613	    # pending-status/numeric-mismatch differences must NEVER be
614	    # silently flattened to convergence regardless of how high
615	    # the token overlap scores. Pure Jaccard treats "X" and
616	    # "not X" as 0.667 similar.
617	    forced_divergence = _force_divergence([f.value for f in nonempty])
618	
619	    if (not forced_divergence) and min_sim >= convergence_floor:
620	        verdict = FieldVerdict(
621	            field_name=field_name,
622	            verdict="convergence",
623	            jurisdictions=tuple(juris_sorted),
624	            bound_ev_ids=ev_ids_sorted,
625	            similarity=min_sim,
626	        )
627	        return verdict, _emit_convergence_paragraph(field_name, nonempty)
628	
629	    verdict = FieldVerdict(
630	        field_name=field_name,
631	        verdict="divergence",
632	        jurisdictions=tuple(juris_sorted),
633	        bound_ev_ids=ev_ids_sorted,
634	        similarity=min_sim,
635	    )
636	    return verdict, _emit_divergence_paragraph(field_name, nonempty)
637	
638	
639	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
640	# Public API
641	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
642	
643	
644	def synthesize_cross_jurisdiction(
645	    findings: list[JurisdictionFinding],
646	    convergence_floor: float = _DEFAULT_CONVERGENCE_FLOOR,
647	) -> CrossJurisdictionSynthesis:
648	    """Group findings by field_name and emit per-field cross-
649	    jurisdiction synthesis.
650	
651	    Returns a CrossJurisdictionSynthesis with one paragraph per
652	    field that has at least one non-empty finding, plus a
653	    structured `verdicts` tuple parallel to the paragraphs.
654	
655	    Raises ValueError on unknown jurisdiction strings to surface
656	    upstream catalog drift loudly (LAW II — never silently
657	    classify "FRD" as "FDA" via fuzzy matching).
658	    """
659	    if not (0.0 <= convergence_floor <= 1.0):
660	        raise ValueError(
661	            f"convergence_floor must be in [0,1]; got {convergence_floor}"
662	        )
663	    for f in findings:
664	        if not is_known_jurisdiction(f.jurisdiction):
665	            raise ValueError(
666	                f"unknown jurisdiction: {f.jurisdiction!r}; "
667	                f"add to _KNOWN_JURISDICTIONS or fix the upstream tag"
668	            )
669	
670	    # Group by canonical field_name (case-insensitive).
671	    groups: dict[str, list[JurisdictionFinding]] = {}
672	    for f in findings:
673	        key = f.field_name.strip().lower()
674	        if not key:
675	            continue
676	        groups.setdefault(key, []).append(f)
677	
678	    paragraphs: list[str] = []
679	    verdicts: list[FieldVerdict] = []
680	    # Stable ordering: alphabetical by field_name.
681	    for field_name in sorted(groups):
682	        verdict, para = _verdict_for_field(
683	            field_name, groups[field_name], convergence_floor,
684	        )
685	        verdicts.append(verdict)
686	        if para:
687	            paragraphs.append(para)
688	
689	    return CrossJurisdictionSynthesis(
690	        paragraphs=tuple(paragraphs),
691	        verdicts=tuple(verdicts),
692	    )
693

content.text: Clean. All 10 files in this shard reviewed. No unprofessional names found — everything is consistent, professional naming (project-specific jargon like "M-63", "I-deepfix-001", "Codex" review references are process/ticket tracking labels, not code names, and appear only in docstrings/comments, not as identifiers). Now writing the TSV output.
